USERMESSAGE #8
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→# OhMyDoc — QA Regression Suite
     2→
     3→Playwright end-to-end tests for [OhMyDoc](https://ohmydoc.vercel.app), an AI-powered document formatter.
     4→
     5→## What's tested
     6→
     7→| Group | Scenarios |
     8→|-------|-----------|
     9→| **1. Basic Load** | Page title, textarea visible, Format button disabled when empty, placeholder text, no console errors, button enables with sufficient input |
    10→| **2. Happy Path — Resume** | Full resume formatting flow, loading state, preview, no raw XML tags, content preservation, HTML structure in output |
    11→| **3. Happy Path — Cover Letter** | Cover letter formatting, content verification |
    12→| **4. Mutation Warning** | DOM inspection for divergence/mutation warning elements, formatted content assertion |
    13→| **5. PDF Export** | Export button disabled before formatting, enabled after, `window.print()` triggered |
    14→| **6. Validation** | Empty and short input keep Format button disabled |
    15→| **7. Skeleton Loading** | Shimmer appears during load, disappears on completion |
    16→| **8. Sample Content** | Sample content availability (button or placeholder), formatting of sample-like content |
    17→
    18→**Total: 16 tests** across 8 feature areas (test 8b skips automatically when no dedicated sample button is present).
    19→
    20→## Requirements
    21→
    22→- Node.js 18+
    23→- Chromium (installed automatically by Playwright)
    24→
    25→## Setup
    26→
    27→```bash
    28→npm install
    29→npx playwright install chromium
    30→```
    31→
    32→## Running tests
    33→
    34→```bash
    35→# Run all tests
    36→npm test
    37→
    38→# Run with visible browser
    39→npx playwright test --headed
    40→
    41→# Run a specific test group
    42→npx playwright test --grep "8"
    43→
    44→# Open HTML report after a run
    45→npm run test:report
    46→```
    47→
    48→## Configuration
    49→
    50→Tests run against `https://ohmydoc.vercel.app` by default.
    51→
    52→Override with the `BASE_URL` environment variable:
    53→
    54→```bash
    55→BASE_URL=https://staging.ohmydoc.vercel.app npx playwright test
    56→```
    57→
    58→Or create a `.env` file:
    59→
    60→```
    61→BASE_URL=https://ohmydoc.vercel.app
    62→```
    63→
    64→## CI notes
    65→
    66→- GitHub Actions workflow at `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — runs on push, PR, and weekly schedule
    67→- Tests run serially (1 worker) — the AI formatting API must not be rate-limited by parallel calls
    68→- AI-dependent tests use a 90s timeout with fast-fail on API errors
    69→- `test.slow()` is set on tests that involve AI calls (expected ~10–20s each)
    70→- Screenshots are saved on failure under `test-results/`
    71→- HTML report is saved as a CI artifact under `playwright-report/`
    72→- Override the target URL via the `BASE_URL` repository variable in GitHub Actions
    73→
    74→## Known observations
    75→
    76→- **No "Load Sample" button**: Sample content is shown as textarea placeholder text only. Test 8b skips cleanly; tests 8a/8c adapt accordingly. A dedicated "Try Sample" button would improve first-visit UX.
    77→- **Mutation warning**: A divergence banner appears after formatting (observed in test 4) — this is expected behavior when AI rewrites content significantly (>5% divergence).
    78→- **Error phrase detection**: `waitForFormattingOutcome` only fast-fails on API key configuration phrases, not generic words like "error" or "failed", to avoid false positives on normal UI text.
    79→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

USERMESSAGE #16
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→import { test, expect, Page } from '@playwright/test';
     2→
     3→const BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'https://ohmydoc.vercel.app';
     4→
     5→const RESUME_TEXT = `
     6→Alex Rivera
     7→alex.rivera@email.com · (555) 123-4567 · San Francisco, CA · linkedin.com/in/alexrivera
     8→
     9→SUMMARY
    10→Full-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.
    11→Passionate about clean code, user experience, and developer tooling.
    12→
    13→EXPERIENCE
    14→
    15→Senior Software Engineer — Acme Corp (2021–Present)
    16→• Led development of a microservices architecture serving 2M+ daily active users
    17→• Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and database query optimization
    18→• Mentored a team of 4 junior engineers and conducted weekly code reviews
    19→
    20→Software Engineer — Bright Labs (2019–2021)
    21→• Built React-based dashboard used by 500+ enterprise clients
    22→• Integrated third-party payment APIs (Stripe, PayPal) handling $10M/month in transactions
    23→• Wrote comprehensive unit and integration tests achieving 90% code coverage
    24→
    25→EDUCATION
    26→B.S. Computer Science — University of California, Berkeley (2019)
    27→Dean's List, GPA 3.8/4.0
    28→
    29→SKILLS
    30→Languages: JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go
    31→Frameworks: React, Node.js, Express, Django
    32→Tools: Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, PostgreSQL, Redis, Git
    33→`.trim();
    34→
    35→const COVER_LETTER_TEXT = `
    36→Alex Rivera
    37→alex.rivera@email.com · San Francisco, CA
    38→
    39→March 4, 2026
    40→
    41→Hiring Manager
    42→TechVision Inc.
    43→123 Innovation Drive
    44→San Francisco, CA 94102
    45→
    46→Dear Hiring Manager,
    47→
    48→I am writing to express my strong interest in the Senior Software Engineer position at TechVision Inc.
    49→With seven years of experience building high-performance web applications and a proven track record
    50→of delivering impactful engineering solutions, I am confident I would make a valuable addition to your team.
    51→
    52→In my current role at Acme Corp, I architected a distributed microservices system that scaled to serve
    53→over two million daily active users while maintaining 99.9% uptime. I thrive in collaborative environments
    54→and enjoy mentoring junior developers to help them grow their technical skills and professional confidence.
    55→
    56→TechVision's commitment to innovation in AI-assisted developer tooling aligns perfectly with my passion
    57→for improving developer experience. I am particularly excited about the opportunity to contribute to
    58→your next-generation IDE features.
    59→
    60→Thank you for considering my application. I look forward to discussing how my background and skills
    61→can contribute to TechVision's continued success.
    62→
    63→Sincerely,
    64→Alex Rivera
    65→`.trim();
    66→
    67→// ─── Helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    68→
    69→async function getTextarea(page: Page) {
    70→  return page.locator('textarea');
    71→}
    72→
    73→/**
    74→ * Reliably fill a textarea and wait for Vue's reactive model to update.
    75→ * Uses click + fill + value verification to avoid intermittent fill() issues.
    76→ */
    77→async function fillTextarea(page: Page, text: string): Promise<void> {
    78→  const textarea = page.locator('textarea');
    79→  await textarea.click();
    80→  await textarea.fill(text);
    81→  // Wait for Vue reactivity to process the input event
    82→  await page.waitForTimeout(300);
    83→  // Verify the value was actually set
    84→  const value = await textarea.inputValue();
    85→  if (value.length < 10 && text.length > 10) {
    86→    // Retry once if fill didn't work
    87→    await textarea.click();
    88→    await textarea.fill(text);
    89→    await page.waitForTimeout(500);
    90→  }
    91→}
    92→
    93→async function getFormatButton(page: Page) {
    94→  return page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });
    95→}
    96→
    97→/**
    98→ * Returns the sample content button/link if the product provides one.
    99→ * OhMyDoc currently shows sample content in the textarea placeholder only;
   100→ * a dedicated button would be the preferred UX pattern.
   101→ */
   102→async function getSampleButton(page: Page) {
   103→  return page.getByRole('button', { name: /sample/i })
   104→    .or(page.getByRole('link', { name: /sample/i }))
   105→    .or(page.getByText(/try sample|load sample|use sample|sample resume/i));
   106→}
   107→
   108→/**
   109→ * Wait for AI formatting to complete OR for an error state to appear.
   110→ * Returns 'success' | 'api_error' | 'timeout'.
   111→ * Uses a short-circuit: if error text appears within 15s we don't wait 90s.
   112→ * @param successText Optional text to look for in the preview to confirm success.
   113→ *                    Defaults to 'Alex Rivera'. Pass null to use generic detection.
   114→ */
   115→async function waitForFormattingOutcome(
   116→  page: Page,
   117→  successText: string | null = 'Alex Rivera'
   118→): Promise<'success' | 'api_error' | 'timeout'> {
   119→  const deadline = Date.now() + 90_000;
   120→  // Keep phrases specific to real API configuration errors, not generic UI words
   121→  const errorPhrases = [
   122→    'api key is not configured',
   123→    'api key not configured',
   124→    'openai api key',
   125→    'not configured',
   126→  ];
   127→
   128→  while (Date.now() < deadline) {
   129→    // Check for success
   130→    if (successText) {
   131→      const textVisible = await page.getByText(successText, { exact: false }).isVisible().catch(() => false);
   132→      if (textVisible) return 'success';
   133→    } else {
   134→      // Generic check: placeholder gone + Format button back to idle state
   135→      const placeholderGone = !(await page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.').isVisible().catch(() => true));
   136→      const btnIdle = await page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i }).isVisible().catch(() => false);
   137→      if (placeholderGone && btnIdle) return 'success';
   138→    }
   139→
   140→    // Check for API error fast
   141→    const bodyText = (await page.locator('body').innerText().catch(() => '')).toLowerCase();
   142→    if (errorPhrases.some(phrase => bodyText.includes(phrase))) {
   143→      return 'api_error';
   144→    }
   145→
   146→    await page.waitForTimeout(1000);
   147→  }
   148→  return 'timeout';
   149→}
   150→
   151→// ─── 1. Basic Load ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   152→
   153→test('1a. Page title is OhMyDoc', async ({ page }) => {
   154→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   155→  await expect(page).toHaveTitle(/OhMyDoc/i);
   156→});
   157→
   158→test('1b. Textarea is visible and accepts input', async ({ page }) => {
   159→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   160→  const textarea = await getTextarea(page);
   161→  await expect(textarea).toBeVisible();
   162→  await textarea.fill('test input');
   163→  await expect(textarea).toHaveValue('test input');
   164→});
   165→
   166→test('1c. Format button is disabled when textarea is empty', async ({ page }) => {
   167→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   168→  const textarea = await getTextarea(page);
   169→  await expect(textarea).toHaveValue('');
   170→  const btn = await getFormatButton(page);
   171→  await expect(btn).toBeDisabled();
   172→});
   173→
   174→test('1d. Right panel shows placeholder text initially', async ({ page }) => {
   175→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   176→  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).toBeVisible();
   177→});
   178→
   179→test('1e. No critical console errors on load', async ({ page }) => {
   180→  const errors: string[] = [];
   181→  page.on('console', msg => {
   182→    if (msg.type() === 'error') errors.push(msg.text());
   183→  });
   184→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   185→  await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle');
   186→  const critical = errors.filter(e =>
   187→    !e.includes('favicon') &&
   188→    !e.includes('net::ERR') &&
   189→    !e.includes('ERR_BLOCKED') &&
   190→    !e.includes('extension')
   191→  );
   192→  expect(critical, `Critical console errors: ${critical.join(', ')}`).toHaveLength(0);
   193→});
   194→
   195→test('1f. Format button enables when sufficient content is entered', async ({ page }) => {
   196→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   197→  const textarea = await getTextarea(page);
   198→  const btn = await getFormatButton(page);
   199→
   200→  // Start with empty — should be disabled
   201→  await expect(btn).toBeDisabled();
   202→
   203→  // Fill with a meaningful amount of text (well over 50 chars)
   204→  await textarea.fill(
   205→    'Jane Smith · jane@example.com · Toronto, ON

Software engineer with 5 years of experience in full-stack web development.'
   206→  );
   207→  await page.waitForTimeout(300); // allow Vue reactivity
   208→
   209→  await expect(btn, 'Format button should be enabled after entering sufficient content').toBeEnabled({ timeout: 3_000 });
   210→});
   211→
   212→// ─── 2. Happy Path — Resume ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
   213→
   214→test('2. Happy path — resume formatting', async ({ page }) => {
   215→  test.slow();
   216→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   217→
   218→  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);
   219→
   220→  const formatBtn = await getFormatButton(page);
   221→  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled();
   222→  await formatBtn.click();
   223→
   224→  // Button should show loading state
   225→  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /formatting/i })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 });
   226→
   227→  const outcome = await waitForFormattingOutcome(page);
   228→
   229→  // If API key is missing, fail with a clear message
   230→  if (outcome === 'api_error') {
   231→    const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();
   232→    const hasApiKeyError = /api key is not configured|api key not configured|not configured/i.test(bodyText);
   233→    if (hasApiKeyError) {
   234→      throw new Error(
   235→        '[BUG] Deployment misconfiguration: "OpenAI API key is not configured." ' +
   236→        'The Vercel deployment is missing the required AI API key environment variable. ' +
   237→        'AI formatting cannot function.'
   238→      );
   239→    }
   240→    throw new Error(`AI formatting returned an error. Page text: ${bodyText.substring(0, 300)}`);
   241→  }
   242→
   243→  expect(outcome, 'Formatting timed out after 90s').toBe('success');
   244→
   245→  // Name should be visible
   246→  await expect(page.getByText('Alex Rivera', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();
   247→
   248→  // No raw XML tags in visible content
   249→  const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();
   250→  expect(bodyText, 'Raw XML tags found in rendered preview').not.toMatch(/<\/?[a-z][^>]*>/);
   251→
   252→  // Key resume content should be preserved in the formatted output
   253→  expect(bodyText, 'Employer "Acme Corp" should be preserved in formatted output').toMatch(/Acme Corp/i);
   254→  expect(bodyText, 'Education should be preserved in formatted output').toMatch(/Berkeley|University of California/i);
   255→
   256→  // Preview should render HTML markup (headings, lists) — not just plain text
   257→  const pageHtml = await page.content();
   258→  const hasHtmlStructure = /<(h[1-6]|strong|b|ul|ol|li)\b/i.test(pageHtml);
   259→  expect(hasHtmlStructure, 'Formatted preview should render HTML structure (headings/lists), not plain text').toBe(true);
   260→
   261→  // Button should return to normal
   262→  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });
   263→});
   264→
   265→// ─── 3. Happy Path — Cover Letter ───────────────────────────────────────────
   266→
   267→test('3. Happy path — cover letter formatting', async ({ page }) => {
   268→  test.slow();
   269→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   270→
   271→  await fillTextarea(page, COVER_LETTER_TEXT);
   272→
   273→  const formatBtn = await getFormatButton(page);
   274→  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled();
   275→  await formatBtn.click();
   276→
   277→  // Use generic detection: placeholder gone + Format button back to idle
   278→  const outcome = await waitForFormattingOutcome(page, null);
   279→
   280→  if (outcome === 'api_error') {
   281→    const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();
   282→    const hasApiKeyError = /api key is not configured|api key not configured|not configured/i.test(bodyText);
   283→    if (hasApiKeyError) {
   284→      throw new Error(
   285→        '[BUG] Deployment misconfiguration: "OpenAI API key is not configured." ' +
   286→        'Cover letter formatting cannot function.'
   287→      );
   288→    }
   289→    throw new Error(`AI formatting returned an error. Page text: ${bodyText.substring(0, 300)}`);
   290→  }
   291→
   292→  expect(outcome, 'Cover letter formatting timed out after 90s').toBe('success');
   293→
   294→  // Verify the formatted preview is visible (not placeholder) and TechVision appears
   295→  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible();
   296→  await expect(page.getByText('TechVision', { exact: false })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 });
   297→
   298→  const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();
   299→  expect(bodyText, 'Raw XML tags found in rendered preview').not.toMatch(/<\/?[a-z][^>]*>/);
   300→});
   301→
   302→// ─── 4. Mutation Warning ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   303→
   304→test('4. Mutation/divergence warning element exists in DOM', async ({ page }) => {
   305→  test.slow();
   306→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   307→
   308→  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);
   309→  await (await getFormatButton(page)).click();
   310→
   311→  const outcome = await waitForFormattingOutcome(page);
   312→
   313→  if (outcome === 'api_error') {
   314→    const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();
   315→    if (/api key is not configured|not configured/i.test(bodyText)) {
   316→      throw new Error(
   317→        '[BUG] Deployment misconfiguration: Cannot test divergence warning — API key not configured.'
   318→      );
   319→    }
   320→  }
   321→
   322→  if (outcome !== 'success') {
   323→    throw new Error(`Formatting did not complete successfully (outcome: ${outcome})`);
   324→  }
   325→
   326→  // Check for warning/alert elements in DOM (may be hidden)
   327→  const warningSelectors = [
   328→    '[class*="warning"]',
   329→    '[class*="diverge"]',
   330→    '[class*="mutation"]',
   331→    '[role="alert"]',
   332→    '[class*="yellow"]',
   333→    '[class*="caution"]',
   334→    '[class*="banner"]',
   335→  ];
   336→
   337→  let warningFound = false;
   338→  for (const selector of warningSelectors) {
   339→    if (await page.locator(selector).count() > 0) {
   340→      warningFound = true;
   341→      break;
   342→    }
   343→  }
   344→  const html = await page.content();
   345→  const hasDivergenceText = /diverge|mutation|content may have been changed|modified/i.test(html);
   346→
   347→  // Informational log — warning only appears when AI divergence exceeds threshold
   348→  console.log(`Divergence/mutation elements found in DOM: ${warningFound || hasDivergenceText}`);
   349→
   350→  // Real assertion: formatted content is present and not an error state
   351→  const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();
   352→  expect(bodyText, '"Alex Rivera" should appear in formatted output').toContain('Alex Rivera');
   353→  expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible();
   354→});
   355→
   356→// ─── 5. PDF Export ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   357→
   358→test('5. PDF Export button triggers window.print()', async ({ page }) => {
   359→  test.slow();
   360→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   361→
   362→  // Export PDF button should be disabled initially
   363→  const exportBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /export pdf/i });
   364→  await expect(exportBtn).toBeDisabled();
   365→
   366→  // Format a document first
   367→  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);
   368→  await (await getFormatButton(page)).click();
   369→
   370→  const outcome = await waitForFormattingOutcome(page);
   371→
   372→  if (outcome === 'api_error') {
   373→    const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();
   374→    if (/api key is not configured|not configured/i.test(bodyText)) {
   375→      throw new Error(
   376→        '[BUG] Deployment misconfiguration: Cannot test PDF Export — API key not configured. ' +
   377→        'Export PDF button remains disabled because formatting never completes.'
   378→      );
   379→    }
   380→  }
   381→
   382→  expect(outcome, 'Formatting timed out before PDF export test').toBe('success');
   383→
   384→  // Export PDF button should now be enabled
   385→  await expect(exportBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 10_000 });
   386→
   387→  // Intercept window.print()
   388→  let printCalled = false;
   389→  await page.exposeFunction('__testPrintCalled', () => { printCalled = true; });
   390→  await page.evaluate(() => {
   391→    window.print = () => {
   392→      (window as any).__testPrintCalled();
   393→    };
   394→  });
   395→
   396→  await exportBtn.click();
   397→  await page.waitForTimeout(1000);
   398→  expect(printCalled, 'window.print() was not called when Export PDF was clicked').toBe(true);
   399→});
   400→
   401→// ─── 6. Error / Validation States ────────────────────────────────────────────
   402→
   403→test('6a. Empty textarea keeps Format button disabled', async ({ page }) => {
   404→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   405→  const btn = await getFormatButton(page);
   406→  await expect(btn).toBeDisabled();
   407→  const textarea = await getTextarea(page);
   408→  await expect(textarea).toHaveValue('');
   409→  await expect(btn).toBeDisabled();
   410→});
   411→
   412→test('6b. Short input (<50 chars) shows validation error or keeps button disabled', async ({ page }) => {
   413→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   414→  const textarea = await getTextarea(page);
   415→  await textarea.fill('hello world test');
   416→
   417→  const btn = await getFormatButton(page);
   418→  if (await btn.isEnabled()) {
   419→    await btn.click();
   420→    // Should show a validation/error message
   421→    await page.waitForTimeout(2000);
   422→    const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();
   423→    const hasValidationMsg = /too short|minimum|at least|characters|please provide/i.test(bodyText);
   424→    const errorCount = await page.locator('[class*="error"], [role="alert"]').count();
   425→    expect(
   426→      hasValidationMsg || errorCount > 0,
   427→      `Expected validation message for short input, body text: ${bodyText.substring(0, 200)}`
   428→    ).toBeTruthy();
   429→  } else {
   430→    // Button disabled for short text — also valid behaviour
   431→    await expect(btn).toBeDisabled();
   432→  }
   433→});
   434→
   435→// ─── 7. Skeleton Loading ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   436→
   437→test('7. Skeleton appears during loading and disappears after', async ({ page }) => {
   438→  test.slow();
   439→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   440→
   441→  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);
   442→  await (await getFormatButton(page)).click();
   443→
   444→  // Check for skeleton/shimmer/pulse shortly after clicking
   445→  const skeletonLocator = page.locator(
   446→    '[class*="skeleton"], [class*="shimmer"], [class*="animate-pulse"], [class*="loading"]'
   447→  );
   448→
   449→  const skeletonAppearedOrMissed = await skeletonLocator.first().isVisible({ timeout: 10_000 }).catch(() => false);
   450→  console.log(`Skeleton visible during loading: ${skeletonAppearedOrMissed}`);
   451→
   452→  const outcome = await waitForFormattingOutcome(page);
   453→
   454→  if (outcome === 'api_error') {
   455→    const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();
   456→    if (/api key is not configured|not configured/i.test(bodyText)) {
   457→      throw new Error(
   458→        '[BUG] Deployment misconfiguration: Cannot fully test skeleton lifecycle — API key not configured. ' +
   459→        `Skeleton appeared during loading: ${skeletonAppearedOrMissed}`
   460→      );
   461→    }
   462→  }
   463→
   464→  expect(outcome, 'Formatting timed out before skeleton disappear check').toBe('success');
   465→
   466→  // After completion, skeleton should not be visible
   467→  const skeletonVisible = await skeletonLocator.first().isVisible().catch(() => false);
   468→  expect(skeletonVisible, 'Skeleton still visible after formatting completed').toBe(false);
   469→});
   470→
   471→// ─── 8. Sample Content ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   472→
   473→/**
   474→ * OhMyDoc provides sample content either via a dedicated "Load Sample" button
   475→ * (preferred UX) or as textarea placeholder text showing an example resume.
   476→ * These tests cover both patterns.
   477→ */
   478→
   479→test('8a. Sample content is available to first-time visitors', async ({ page }) => {
   480→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   481→
   482→  const sampleBtn = await getSampleButton(page);
   483→  const hasSampleBtn = await sampleBtn.first().isVisible({ timeout: 3_000 }).catch(() => false);
   484→
   485→  if (hasSampleBtn) {
   486→    console.log('Sample content: dedicated button found');
   487→    expect(hasSampleBtn).toBe(true);
   488→    return;
   489→  }
   490→
   491→  // Fallback: check for example/sample content in textarea placeholder
   492→  const textarea = await getTextarea(page);
   493→  const placeholder = await textarea.getAttribute('placeholder');
   494→  const hasExampleInPlaceholder = /example|jane smith|resume|software engineer/i.test(placeholder ?? '');
   495→  console.log(`Sample content: placeholder shows example? ${hasExampleInPlaceholder}. Placeholder: "${placeholder?.substring(0, 100)}"`);
   496→
   497→  // Also check page HTML for any sample-related elements
   498→  const html = await page.content();
   499→  const hasSampleInHtml = /sample|try it|load example/i.test(html);
   500→
   501→  expect(
   502→    hasSampleBtn || hasExampleInPlaceholder || hasSampleInHtml,
   503→    'Expected either a sample button OR example content in textarea placeholder'
   504→  ).toBeTruthy();
   505→});
   506→
   507→test('8b. Sample content button populates textarea and enables Format button', async ({ page }) => {
   508→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   509→
   510→  const sampleBtn = await getSampleButton(page);
   511→  const hasSampleBtn = await sampleBtn.first().isVisible({ timeout: 3_000 }).catch(() => false);
   512→
   513→  if (!hasSampleBtn) {
   514→    // No dedicated sample button — skip this button-specific test
   515→    // Sample content availability is already verified in 8a via placeholder
   516→    console.log('[OBSERVATION] No dedicated "Load Sample" button found. Skipping button interaction test.');
   517→    console.log('Recommendation: add a "Try Sample" button so users can load sample content with one click.');
   518→    test.skip();
   519→    return;
   520→  }
   521→
   522→  await sampleBtn.first().click();
   523→
   524→  // Textarea should now have content
   525→  const textarea = await getTextarea(page);
   526→  const value = await textarea.inputValue();
   527→  expect(value.length, 'Sample content did not populate the textarea').toBeGreaterThan(50);
   528→
   529→  // Format button should be enabled after loading sample
   530→  const formatBtn = await getFormatButton(page);
   531→  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });
   532→});
   533→
   534→test('8c. Sample content (pasted manually from placeholder) can be formatted end-to-end', async ({ page }) => {
   535→  test.slow();
   536→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   537→
   538→  // Try dedicated sample button first
   539→  const sampleBtn = await getSampleButton(page);
   540→  const hasSampleBtn = await sampleBtn.first().isVisible({ timeout: 3_000 }).catch(() => false);
   541→
   542→  const textarea = await getTextarea(page);
   543→
   544→  if (hasSampleBtn) {
   545→    await sampleBtn.first().click();
   546→    const value = await textarea.inputValue();
   547→    expect(value.length).toBeGreaterThan(50);
   548→  } else {
   549→    // Use the sample resume text as a proxy for what a first-time user would paste
   550→    const sampleContent = `Jane Smith
   551→jane@email.com · Toronto, ON
   552→
   553→Software Engineer with 8 years of experience in full-stack development.
   554→Skilled in React, Node.js, Python, and cloud infrastructure.
   555→
   556→EXPERIENCE
   557→Senior Engineer — CloudTech Inc (2020–Present)
   558→• Built scalable APIs serving 1M+ requests per day
   559→• Reduced infrastructure costs by 30% through optimization
   560→
   561→EDUCATION
   562→B.Sc. Computer Science — University of Toronto (2016)
   563→
   564→SKILLS
   565→JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, React, Node.js, AWS, Docker`;
   566→    await fillTextarea(page, sampleContent);
   567→    console.log('[OBSERVATION] No "Load Sample" button found — using hardcoded sample text to test formatting flow.');
   568→  }
   569→
   570→  // Click format
   571→  const formatBtn = await getFormatButton(page);
   572→  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled();
   573→  await formatBtn.click();
   574→
   575→  // Wait for outcome — use generic detection (no fixed name to look for)
   576→  const outcome = await waitForFormattingOutcome(page, null);
   577→
   578→  if (outcome === 'api_error') {
   579→    const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();
   580→    if (/api key is not configured|not configured/i.test(bodyText)) {
   581→      throw new Error(
   582→        '[BUG] Deployment misconfiguration: Sample content formatting failed — API key not configured.'
   583→      );
   584→    }
   585→    throw new Error(`Sample content formatting returned an error. Page text: ${bodyText.substring(0, 300)}`);
   586→  }
   587→
   588→  expect(outcome, 'Sample content formatting timed out after 90s').toBe('success');
   589→
   590→  // Preview should show formatted content (not placeholder)
   591→  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });
   592→});
   593→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

{
  "content": {
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_012Ra2ePHLH3ARxHrErPQFsv",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192import { test, expect, Page } from '@playwright/test';\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192const BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'https://ohmydoc.vercel.app';\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192const RESUME_TEXT = `\n     6\u2192Alex Rivera\n     7\u2192alex.rivera@email.com \u00b7 (555) 123-4567 \u00b7 San Francisco, CA \u00b7 linkedin.com/in/alexrivera\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192SUMMARY\n    10\u2192Full-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.\n    11\u2192Passionate about clean code, user experience, and developer tooling.\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192EXPERIENCE\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192Senior Software Engineer \u2014 Acme Corp (2021\u2013Present)\n    16\u2192\u2022 Led development of a microservices architecture serving 2M+ daily active users\n    17\u2192\u2022 Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and database query optimization\n    18\u2192\u2022 Mentored a team of 4 junior engineers and conducted weekly code reviews\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192Software Engineer \u2014 Bright Labs (2019\u20132021)\n    21\u2192\u2022 Built React-based dashboard used by 500+ enterprise clients\n    22\u2192\u2022 Integrated third-party payment APIs (Stripe, PayPal) handling $10M/month in transactions\n    23\u2192\u2022 Wrote comprehensive unit and integration tests achieving 90% code coverage\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192EDUCATION\n    26\u2192B.S. Computer Science \u2014 University of California, Berkeley (2019)\n    27\u2192Dean's List, GPA 3.8/4.0\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192SKILLS\n    30\u2192Languages: JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go\n    31\u2192Frameworks: React, Node.js, Express, Django\n    32\u2192Tools: Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, PostgreSQL, Redis, Git\n    33\u2192`.trim();\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192const COVER_LETTER_TEXT = `\n    36\u2192Alex Rivera\n    37\u2192alex.rivera@email.com \u00b7 San Francisco, CA\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192March 4, 2026\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192Hiring Manager\n    42\u2192TechVision Inc.\n    43\u2192123 Innovation Drive\n    44\u2192San Francisco, CA 94102\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192Dear Hiring Manager,\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192I am writing to express my strong interest in the Senior Software Engineer position at TechVision Inc.\n    49\u2192With seven years of experience building high-performance web applications and a proven track record\n    50\u2192of delivering impactful engineering solutions, I am confident I would make a valuable addition to your team.\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192In my current role at Acme Corp, I architected a distributed microservices system that scaled to serve\n    53\u2192over two million daily active users while maintaining 99.9% uptime. I thrive in collaborative environments\n    54\u2192and enjoy mentoring junior developers to help them grow their technical skills and professional confidence.\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192TechVision's commitment to innovation in AI-assisted developer tooling aligns perfectly with my passion\n    57\u2192for improving developer experience. I am particularly excited about the opportunity to contribute to\n    58\u2192your next-generation IDE features.\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192Thank you for considering my application. I look forward to discussing how my background and skills\n    61\u2192can contribute to TechVision's continued success.\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192Sincerely,\n    64\u2192Alex Rivera\n    65\u2192`.trim();\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192async function getTextarea(page: Page) {\n    70\u2192  return page.locator('textarea');\n    71\u2192}\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192/**\n    74\u2192 * Reliably fill a textarea and wait for Vue's reactive model to update.\n    75\u2192 * Uses click + fill + value verification to avoid intermittent fill() issues.\n    76\u2192 */\n    77\u2192async function fillTextarea(page: Page, text: string): Promise<void> {\n    78\u2192  const textarea = page.locator('textarea');\n    79\u2192  await textarea.click();\n    80\u2192  await textarea.fill(text);\n    81\u2192  // Wait for Vue reactivity to process the input event\n    82\u2192  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n    83\u2192  // Verify the value was actually set\n    84\u2192  const value = await textarea.inputValue();\n    85\u2192  if (value.length < 10 && text.length > 10) {\n    86\u2192    // Retry once if fill didn't work\n    87\u2192    await textarea.click();\n    88\u2192    await textarea.fill(text);\n    89\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n    90\u2192  }\n    91\u2192}\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192async function getFormatButton(page: Page) {\n    94\u2192  return page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n    95\u2192}\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192/**\n    98\u2192 * Returns the sample content button/link if the product provides one.\n    99\u2192 * OhMyDoc currently shows sample content in the textarea placeholder only;\n   100\u2192 * a dedicated button would be the preferred UX pattern.\n   101\u2192 */\n   102\u2192async function getSampleButton(page: Page) {\n   103\u2192  return page.getByRole('button', { name: /sample/i })\n   104\u2192    .or(page.getByRole('link', { name: /sample/i }))\n   105\u2192    .or(page.getByText(/try sample|load sample|use sample|sample resume/i));\n   106\u2192}\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192/**\n   109\u2192 * Wait for AI formatting to complete OR for an error state to appear.\n   110\u2192 * Returns 'success' | 'api_error' | 'timeout'.\n   111\u2192 * Uses a short-circuit: if error text appears within 15s we don't wait 90s.\n   112\u2192 * @param successText Optional text to look for in the preview to confirm success.\n   113\u2192 *                    Defaults to 'Alex Rivera'. Pass null to use generic detection.\n   114\u2192 */\n   115\u2192async function waitForFormattingOutcome(\n   116\u2192  page: Page,\n   117\u2192  successText: string | null = 'Alex Rivera'\n   118\u2192): Promise<'success' | 'api_error' | 'timeout'> {\n   119\u2192  const deadline = Date.now() + 90_000;\n   120\u2192  // Keep phrases specific to real API configuration errors, not generic UI words\n   121\u2192  const errorPhrases = [\n   122\u2192    'api key is not configured',\n   123\u2192    'api key not configured',\n   124\u2192    'openai api key',\n   125\u2192    'not configured',\n   126\u2192  ];\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192  while (Date.now() < deadline) {\n   129\u2192    // Check for success\n   130\u2192    if (successText) {\n   131\u2192      const textVisible = await page.getByText(successText, { exact: false }).isVisible().catch(() => false);\n   132\u2192      if (textVisible) return 'success';\n   133\u2192    } else {\n   134\u2192      // Generic check: placeholder gone + Format button back to idle state\n   135\u2192      const placeholderGone = !(await page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.').isVisible().catch(() => true));\n   136\u2192      const btnIdle = await page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i }).isVisible().catch(() => false);\n   137\u2192      if (placeholderGone && btnIdle) return 'success';\n   138\u2192    }\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192    // Check for API error fast\n   141\u2192    const bodyText = (await page.locator('body').innerText().catch(() => '')).toLowerCase();\n   142\u2192    if (errorPhrases.some(phrase => bodyText.includes(phrase))) {\n   143\u2192      return 'api_error';\n   144\u2192    }\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(1000);\n   147\u2192  }\n   148\u2192  return 'timeout';\n   149\u2192}\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 1. Basic Load \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192test('1a. Page title is OhMyDoc', async ({ page }) => {\n   154\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   155\u2192  await expect(page).toHaveTitle(/OhMyDoc/i);\n   156\u2192});\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192test('1b. Textarea is visible and accepts input', async ({ page }) => {\n   159\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   160\u2192  const textarea = await getTextarea(page);\n   161\u2192  await expect(textarea).toBeVisible();\n   162\u2192  await textarea.fill('test input');\n   163\u2192  await expect(textarea).toHaveValue('test input');\n   164\u2192});\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192test('1c. Format button is disabled when textarea is empty', async ({ page }) => {\n   167\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   168\u2192  const textarea = await getTextarea(page);\n   169\u2192  await expect(textarea).toHaveValue('');\n   170\u2192  const btn = await getFormatButton(page);\n   171\u2192  await expect(btn).toBeDisabled();\n   172\u2192});\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192test('1d. Right panel shows placeholder text initially', async ({ page }) => {\n   175\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   176\u2192  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).toBeVisible();\n   177\u2192});\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192test('1e. No critical console errors on load', async ({ page }) => {\n   180\u2192  const errors: string[] = [];\n   181\u2192  page.on('console', msg => {\n   182\u2192    if (msg.type() === 'error') errors.push(msg.text());\n   183\u2192  });\n   184\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   185\u2192  await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle');\n   186\u2192  const critical = errors.filter(e =>\n   187\u2192    !e.includes('favicon') &&\n   188\u2192    !e.includes('net::ERR') &&\n   189\u2192    !e.includes('ERR_BLOCKED') &&\n   190\u2192    !e.includes('extension')\n   191\u2192  );\n   192\u2192  expect(critical, `Critical console errors: ${critical.join(', ')}`).toHaveLength(0);\n   193\u2192});\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192test('1f. Format button enables when sufficient content is entered', async ({ page }) => {\n   196\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   197\u2192  const textarea = await getTextarea(page);\n   198\u2192  const btn = await getFormatButton(page);\n   199\u2192\n   200\u2192  // Start with empty \u2014 should be disabled\n   201\u2192  await expect(btn).toBeDisabled();\n   202\u2192\n   203\u2192  // Fill with a meaningful amount of text (well over 50 chars)\n   204\u2192  await textarea.fill(\n   205\u2192    'Jane Smith \u00b7 jane@example.com \u00b7 Toronto, ON\\n\\nSoftware engineer with 5 years of experience in full-stack web development.'\n   206\u2192  );\n   207\u2192  await page.waitForTimeout(300); // allow Vue reactivity\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192  await expect(btn, 'Format button should be enabled after entering sufficient content').toBeEnabled({ timeout: 3_000 });\n   210\u2192});\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 2. Happy Path \u2014 Resume \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192test('2. Happy path \u2014 resume formatting', async ({ page }) => {\n   215\u2192  test.slow();\n   216\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n   219\u2192\n   220\u2192  const formatBtn = await getFormatButton(page);\n   221\u2192  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled();\n   222\u2192  await formatBtn.click();\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192  // Button should show loading state\n   225\u2192  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /formatting/i })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 });\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192  const outcome = await waitForFormattingOutcome(page);\n   228\u2192\n   229\u2192  // If API key is missing, fail with a clear message\n   230\u2192  if (outcome === 'api_error') {\n   231\u2192    const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();\n   232\u2192    const hasApiKeyError = /api key is not configured|api key not configured|not configured/i.test(bodyText);\n   233\u2192    if (hasApiKeyError) {\n   234\u2192      throw new Error(\n   235\u2192        '[BUG] Deployment misconfiguration: \"OpenAI API key is not configured.\" ' +\n   236\u2192        'The Vercel deployment is missing the required AI API key environment variable. ' +\n   237\u2192        'AI formatting cannot function.'\n   238\u2192      );\n   239\u2192    }\n   240\u2192    throw new Error(`AI formatting returned an error. Page text: ${bodyText.substring(0, 300)}`);\n   241\u2192  }\n   242\u2192\n   243\u2192  expect(outcome, 'Formatting timed out after 90s').toBe('success');\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192  // Name should be visible\n   246\u2192  await expect(page.getByText('Alex Rivera', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192  // No raw XML tags in visible content\n   249\u2192  const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();\n   250\u2192  expect(bodyText, 'Raw XML tags found in rendered preview').not.toMatch(/<\\/?[a-z][^>]*>/);\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192  // Key resume content should be preserved in the formatted output\n   253\u2192  expect(bodyText, 'Employer \"Acme Corp\" should be preserved in formatted output').toMatch(/Acme Corp/i);\n   254\u2192  expect(bodyText, 'Education should be preserved in formatted output').toMatch(/Berkeley|University of California/i);\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192  // Preview should render HTML markup (headings, lists) \u2014 not just plain text\n   257\u2192  const pageHtml = await page.content();\n   258\u2192  const hasHtmlStructure = /<(h[1-6]|strong|b|ul|ol|li)\\b/i.test(pageHtml);\n   259\u2192  expect(hasHtmlStructure, 'Formatted preview should render HTML structure (headings/lists), not plain text').toBe(true);\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192  // Button should return to normal\n   262\u2192  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n   263\u2192});\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 3. Happy Path \u2014 Cover Letter \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   266\u2192\n   267\u2192test('3. Happy path \u2014 cover letter formatting', async ({ page }) => {\n   268\u2192  test.slow();\n   269\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192  await fillTextarea(page, COVER_LETTER_TEXT);\n   272\u2192\n   273\u2192  const formatBtn = await getFormatButton(page);\n   274\u2192  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled();\n   275\u2192  await formatBtn.click();\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192  // Use generic detection: placeholder gone + Format button back to idle\n   278\u2192  const outcome = await waitForFormattingOutcome(page, null);\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192  if (outcome === 'api_error') {\n   281\u2192    const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();\n   282\u2192    const hasApiKeyError = /api key is not configured|api key not configured|not configured/i.test(bodyText);\n   283\u2192    if (hasApiKeyError) {\n   284\u2192      throw new Error(\n   285\u2192        '[BUG] Deployment misconfiguration: \"OpenAI API key is not configured.\" ' +\n   286\u2192        'Cover letter formatting cannot function.'\n   287\u2192      );\n   288\u2192    }\n   289\u2192    throw new Error(`AI formatting returned an error. Page text: ${bodyText.substring(0, 300)}`);\n   290\u2192  }\n   291\u2192\n   292\u2192  expect(outcome, 'Cover letter formatting timed out after 90s').toBe('success');\n   293\u2192\n   294\u2192  // Verify the formatted preview is visible (not placeholder) and TechVision appears\n   295\u2192  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible();\n   296\u2192  await expect(page.getByText('TechVision', { exact: false })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 });\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192  const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();\n   299\u2192  expect(bodyText, 'Raw XML tags found in rendered preview').not.toMatch(/<\\/?[a-z][^>]*>/);\n   300\u2192});\n   301\u2192\n   302\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 4. Mutation Warning \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192test('4. Mutation/divergence warning element exists in DOM', async ({ page }) => {\n   305\u2192  test.slow();\n   306\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   307\u2192\n   308\u2192  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n   309\u2192  await (await getFormatButton(page)).click();\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192  const outcome = await waitForFormattingOutcome(page);\n   312\u2192\n   313\u2192  if (outcome === 'api_error') {\n   314\u2192    const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();\n   315\u2192    if (/api key is not configured|not configured/i.test(bodyText)) {\n   316\u2192      throw new Error(\n   317\u2192        '[BUG] Deployment misconfiguration: Cannot test divergence warning \u2014 API key not configured.'\n   318\u2192      );\n   319\u2192    }\n   320\u2192  }\n   321\u2192\n   322\u2192  if (outcome !== 'success') {\n   323\u2192    throw new Error(`Formatting did not complete successfully (outcome: ${outcome})`);\n   324\u2192  }\n   325\u2192\n   326\u2192  // Check for warning/alert elements in DOM (may be hidden)\n   327\u2192  const warningSelectors = [\n   328\u2192    '[class*=\"warning\"]',\n   329\u2192    '[class*=\"diverge\"]',\n   330\u2192    '[class*=\"mutation\"]',\n   331\u2192    '[role=\"alert\"]',\n   332\u2192    '[class*=\"yellow\"]',\n   333\u2192    '[class*=\"caution\"]',\n   334\u2192    '[class*=\"banner\"]',\n   335\u2192  ];\n   336\u2192\n   337\u2192  let warningFound = false;\n   338\u2192  for (const selector of warningSelectors) {\n   339\u2192    if (await page.locator(selector).count() > 0) {\n   340\u2192      warningFound = true;\n   341\u2192      break;\n   342\u2192    }\n   343\u2192  }\n   344\u2192  const html = await page.content();\n   345\u2192  const hasDivergenceText = /diverge|mutation|content may have been changed|modified/i.test(html);\n   346\u2192\n   347\u2192  // Informational log \u2014 warning only appears when AI divergence exceeds threshold\n   348\u2192  console.log(`Divergence/mutation elements found in DOM: ${warningFound || hasDivergenceText}`);\n   349\u2192\n   350\u2192  // Real assertion: formatted content is present and not an error state\n   351\u2192  const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();\n   352\u2192  expect(bodyText, '\"Alex Rivera\" should appear in formatted output').toContain('Alex Rivera');\n   353\u2192  expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible();\n   354\u2192});\n   355\u2192\n   356\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 5. PDF Export \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   357\u2192\n   358\u2192test('5. PDF Export button triggers window.print()', async ({ page }) => {\n   359\u2192  test.slow();\n   360\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   361\u2192\n   362\u2192  // Export PDF button should be disabled initially\n   363\u2192  const exportBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /export pdf/i });\n   364\u2192  await expect(exportBtn).toBeDisabled();\n   365\u2192\n   366\u2192  // Format a document first\n   367\u2192  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n   368\u2192  await (await getFormatButton(page)).click();\n   369\u2192\n   370\u2192  const outcome = await waitForFormattingOutcome(page);\n   371\u2192\n   372\u2192  if (outcome === 'api_error') {\n   373\u2192    const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();\n   374\u2192    if (/api key is not configured|not configured/i.test(bodyText)) {\n   375\u2192      throw new Error(\n   376\u2192        '[BUG] Deployment misconfiguration: Cannot test PDF Export \u2014 API key not configured. ' +\n   377\u2192        'Export PDF button remains disabled because formatting never completes.'\n   378\u2192      );\n   379\u2192    }\n   380\u2192  }\n   381\u2192\n   382\u2192  expect(outcome, 'Formatting timed out before PDF export test').toBe('success');\n   383\u2192\n   384\u2192  // Export PDF button should now be enabled\n   385\u2192  await expect(exportBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 10_000 });\n   386\u2192\n   387\u2192  // Intercept window.print()\n   388\u2192  let printCalled = false;\n   389\u2192  await page.exposeFunction('__testPrintCalled', () => { printCalled = true; });\n   390\u2192  await page.evaluate(() => {\n   391\u2192    window.print = () => {\n   392\u2192      (window as any).__testPrintCalled();\n   393\u2192    };\n   394\u2192  });\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192  await exportBtn.click();\n   397\u2192  await page.waitForTimeout(1000);\n   398\u2192  expect(printCalled, 'window.print() was not called when Export PDF was clicked').toBe(true);\n   399\u2192});\n   400\u2192\n   401\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 6. Error / Validation States \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   402\u2192\n   403\u2192test('6a. Empty textarea keeps Format button disabled', async ({ page }) => {\n   404\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   405\u2192  const btn = await getFormatButton(page);\n   406\u2192  await expect(btn).toBeDisabled();\n   407\u2192  const textarea = await getTextarea(page);\n   408\u2192  await expect(textarea).toHaveValue('');\n   409\u2192  await expect(btn).toBeDisabled();\n   410\u2192});\n   411\u2192\n   412\u2192test('6b. Short input (<50 chars) shows validation error or keeps button disabled', async ({ page }) => {\n   413\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   414\u2192  const textarea = await getTextarea(page);\n   415\u2192  await textarea.fill('hello world test');\n   416\u2192\n   417\u2192  const btn = await getFormatButton(page);\n   418\u2192  if (await btn.isEnabled()) {\n   419\u2192    await btn.click();\n   420\u2192    // Should show a validation/error message\n   421\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n   422\u2192    const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();\n   423\u2192    const hasValidationMsg = /too short|minimum|at least|characters|please provide/i.test(bodyText);\n   424\u2192    const errorCount = await page.locator('[class*=\"error\"], [role=\"alert\"]').count();\n   425\u2192    expect(\n   426\u2192      hasValidationMsg || errorCount > 0,\n   427\u2192      `Expected validation message for short input, body text: ${bodyText.substring(0, 200)}`\n   428\u2192    ).toBeTruthy();\n   429\u2192  } else {\n   430\u2192    // Button disabled for short text \u2014 also valid behaviour\n   431\u2192    await expect(btn).toBeDisabled();\n   432\u2192  }\n   433\u2192});\n   434\u2192\n   435\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 7. Skeleton Loading \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   436\u2192\n   437\u2192test('7. Skeleton appears during loading and disappears after', async ({ page }) => {\n   438\u2192  test.slow();\n   439\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   440\u2192\n   441\u2192  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n   442\u2192  await (await getFormatButton(page)).click();\n   443\u2192\n   444\u2192  // Check for skeleton/shimmer/pulse shortly after clicking\n   445\u2192  const skeletonLocator = page.locator(\n   446\u2192    '[class*=\"skeleton\"], [class*=\"shimmer\"], [class*=\"animate-pulse\"], [class*=\"loading\"]'\n   447\u2192  );\n   448\u2192\n   449\u2192  const skeletonAppearedOrMissed = await skeletonLocator.first().isVisible({ timeout: 10_000 }).catch(() => false);\n   450\u2192  console.log(`Skeleton visible during loading: ${skeletonAppearedOrMissed}`);\n   451\u2192\n   452\u2192  const outcome = await waitForFormattingOutcome(page);\n   453\u2192\n   454\u2192  if (outcome === 'api_error') {\n   455\u2192    const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();\n   456\u2192    if (/api key is not configured|not configured/i.test(bodyText)) {\n   457\u2192      throw new Error(\n   458\u2192        '[BUG] Deployment misconfiguration: Cannot fully test skeleton lifecycle \u2014 API key not configured. ' +\n   459\u2192        `Skeleton appeared during loading: ${skeletonAppearedOrMissed}`\n   460\u2192      );\n   461\u2192    }\n   462\u2192  }\n   463\u2192\n   464\u2192  expect(outcome, 'Formatting timed out before skeleton disappear check').toBe('success');\n   465\u2192\n   466\u2192  // After completion, skeleton should not be visible\n   467\u2192  const skeletonVisible = await skeletonLocator.first().isVisible().catch(() => false);\n   468\u2192  expect(skeletonVisible, 'Skeleton still visible after formatting completed').toBe(false);\n   469\u2192});\n   470\u2192\n   471\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 8. Sample Content \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   472\u2192\n   473\u2192/**\n   474\u2192 * OhMyDoc provides sample content either via a dedicated \"Load Sample\" button\n   475\u2192 * (preferred UX) or as textarea placeholder text showing an example resume.\n   476\u2192 * These tests cover both patterns.\n   477\u2192 */\n   478\u2192\n   479\u2192test('8a. Sample content is available to first-time visitors', async ({ page }) => {\n   480\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   481\u2192\n   482\u2192  const sampleBtn = await getSampleButton(page);\n   483\u2192  const hasSampleBtn = await sampleBtn.first().isVisible({ timeout: 3_000 }).catch(() => false);\n   484\u2192\n   485\u2192  if (hasSampleBtn) {\n   486\u2192    console.log('Sample content: dedicated button found');\n   487\u2192    expect(hasSampleBtn).toBe(true);\n   488\u2192    return;\n   489\u2192  }\n   490\u2192\n   491\u2192  // Fallback: check for example/sample content in textarea placeholder\n   492\u2192  const textarea = await getTextarea(page);\n   493\u2192  const placeholder = await textarea.getAttribute('placeholder');\n   494\u2192  const hasExampleInPlaceholder = /example|jane smith|resume|software engineer/i.test(placeholder ?? '');\n   495\u2192  console.log(`Sample content: placeholder shows example? ${hasExampleInPlaceholder}. Placeholder: \"${placeholder?.substring(0, 100)}\"`);\n   496\u2192\n   497\u2192  // Also check page HTML for any sample-related elements\n   498\u2192  const html = await page.content();\n   499\u2192  const hasSampleInHtml = /sample|try it|load example/i.test(html);\n   500\u2192\n   501\u2192  expect(\n   502\u2192    hasSampleBtn || hasExampleInPlaceholder || hasSampleInHtml,\n   503\u2192    'Expected either a sample button OR example content in textarea placeholder'\n   504\u2192  ).toBeTruthy();\n   505\u2192});\n   506\u2192\n   507\u2192test('8b. Sample content button populates textarea and enables Format button', async ({ page }) => {\n   508\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   509\u2192\n   510\u2192  const sampleBtn = await getSampleButton(page);\n   511\u2192  const hasSampleBtn = await sampleBtn.first().isVisible({ timeout: 3_000 }).catch(() => false);\n   512\u2192\n   513\u2192  if (!hasSampleBtn) {\n   514\u2192    // No dedicated sample button \u2014 skip this button-specific test\n   515\u2192    // Sample content availability is already verified in 8a via placeholder\n   516\u2192    console.log('[OBSERVATION] No dedicated \"Load Sample\" button found. Skipping button interaction test.');\n   517\u2192    console.log('Recommendation: add a \"Try Sample\" button so users can load sample content with one click.');\n   518\u2192    test.skip();\n   519\u2192    return;\n   520\u2192  }\n   521\u2192\n   522\u2192  await sampleBtn.first().click();\n   523\u2192\n   524\u2192  // Textarea should now have content\n   525\u2192  const textarea = await getTextarea(page);\n   526\u2192  const value = await textarea.inputValue();\n   527\u2192  expect(value.length, 'Sample content did not populate the textarea').toBeGreaterThan(50);\n   528\u2192\n   529\u2192  // Format button should be enabled after loading sample\n   530\u2192  const formatBtn = await getFormatButton(page);\n   531\u2192  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n   532\u2192});\n   533\u2192\n   534\u2192test('8c. Sample content (pasted manually from placeholder) can be formatted end-to-end', async ({ page }) => {\n   535\u2192  test.slow();\n   536\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   537\u2192\n   538\u2192  // Try dedicated sample button first\n   539\u2192  const sampleBtn = await getSampleButton(page);\n   540\u2192  const hasSampleBtn = await sampleBtn.first().isVisible({ timeout: 3_000 }).catch(() => false);\n   541\u2192\n   542\u2192  const textarea = await getTextarea(page);\n   543\u2192\n   544\u2192  if (hasSampleBtn) {\n   545\u2192    await sampleBtn.first().click();\n   546\u2192    const value = await textarea.inputValue();\n   547\u2192    expect(value.length).toBeGreaterThan(50);\n   548\u2192  } else {\n   549\u2192    // Use the sample resume text as a proxy for what a first-time user would paste\n   550\u2192    const sampleContent = `Jane Smith\n   551\u2192jane@email.com \u00b7 Toronto, ON\n   552\u2192\n   553\u2192Software Engineer with 8 years of experience in full-stack development.\n   554\u2192Skilled in React, Node.js, Python, and cloud infrastructure.\n   555\u2192\n   556\u2192EXPERIENCE\n   557\u2192Senior Engineer \u2014 CloudTech Inc (2020\u2013Present)\n   558\u2192\u2022 Built scalable APIs serving 1M+ requests per day\n   559\u2192\u2022 Reduced infrastructure costs by 30% through optimization\n   560\u2192\n   561\u2192EDUCATION\n   562\u2192B.Sc. Computer Science \u2014 University of Toronto (2016)\n   563\u2192\n   564\u2192SKILLS\n   565\u2192JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, React, Node.js, AWS, Docker`;\n   566\u2192    await fillTextarea(page, sampleContent);\n   567\u2192    console.log('[OBSERVATION] No \"Load Sample\" button found \u2014 using hardcoded sample text to test formatting flow.');\n   568\u2192  }\n   569\u2192\n   570\u2192  // Click format\n   571\u2192  const formatBtn = await getFormatButton(page);\n   572\u2192  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled();\n   573\u2192  await formatBtn.click();\n   574\u2192\n   575\u2192  // Wait for outcome \u2014 use generic detection (no fixed name to look for)\n   576\u2192  const outcome = await waitForFormattingOutcome(page, null);\n   577\u2192\n   578\u2192  if (outcome === 'api_error') {\n   579\u2192    const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();\n   580\u2192    if (/api key is not configured|not configured/i.test(bodyText)) {\n   581\u2192      throw new Error(\n   582\u2192        '[BUG] Deployment misconfiguration: Sample content formatting failed \u2014 API key not configured.'\n   583\u2192      );\n   584\u2192    }\n   585\u2192    throw new Error(`Sample content formatting returned an error. Page text: ${bodyText.substring(0, 300)}`);\n   586\u2192  }\n   587\u2192\n   588\u2192  expect(outcome, 'Sample content formatting timed out after 90s').toBe('success');\n   589\u2192\n   590\u2192  // Preview should show formatted content (not placeholder)\n   591\u2192  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n   592\u2192});\n   593\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}
