     1→import { test, expect, Page } from '@playwright/test';
     2→
     3→const BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:3002';
     4→
     5→// ─── Mock XML responses ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     6→
     7→const MOCK_RESUME_XML = `<document type="resume">
     8→  <header>
     9→    <name>Alex Morgan</name>
    10→    <contact>
    11→      <email>alex.morgan@email.com</email>
    12→      <phone>(416) 555-0182</phone>
    13→      <location>Toronto, ON</location>
    14→    </contact>
    15→  </header>
    16→  <summary>Senior Software Engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.</summary>
    17→  <experience>
    18→    <role>
    19→      <title>Senior Software Engineer</title>
    20→      <company>Shopify</company>
    21→      <period>March 2021 – Present</period>
    22→      <bullets>
    23→        <bullet>Led migration of core checkout service from monolith to microservices</bullet>
    24→      </bullets>
    25→    </role>
    26→  </experience>
    27→  <education>
    28→    <degree>Bachelor of Science, Computer Science</degree>
    29→    <school>University of Waterloo</school>
    30→    <year>2016</year>
    31→  </education>
    32→  <skills>JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, React, Node.js, PostgreSQL, AWS</skills>
    33→</document>`;
    34→
    35→const MOCK_LETTER_XML = `<document type="cover-letter">
    36→  <header>
    37→    <name>Jane Park</name>
    38→    <contact>
    39→      <email>jane.park@email.com</email>
    40→      <location>Vancouver, BC</location>
    41→    </contact>
    42→  </header>
    43→  <date>March 9, 2026</date>
    44→  <recipient>
    45→    <name>Hiring Manager</name>
    46→    <company>Acme Technology</company>
    47→    <address>San Francisco, CA</address>
    48→  </recipient>
    49→  <greeting>Dear Hiring Manager,</greeting>
    50→  <body>
    51→    <paragraph>I am writing to apply for the Product Designer position at Acme Technology.</paragraph>
    52→    <paragraph>Thank you for your time and consideration.</paragraph>
    53→  </body>
    54→  <closing>Sincerely,</closing>
    55→  <signature>Jane Park</signature>
    56→</document>`;
    57→
    58→// ─── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    59→
    60→/**
    61→ * Navigate to BASE_URL and wait for full hydration (including Nuxt 3 client-side
    62→ * mounting) before the test body begins.
    63→ */
    64→async function gotoAndWait(page: Page): Promise<void> {
    65→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
    66→  await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle');
    67→  await page.locator('textarea').waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 15_000 });
    68→}
    69→
    70→/**
    71→ * Scope type-toggle buttons to the .type-selector container to avoid matching
    72→ * the "Format My Resume" / "Format My Letter" UButton.
    73→ */
    74→function getResumeToggle(page: Page) {
    75→  return page.locator('button.type-btn').filter({ hasText: 'Resume' });
    76→}
    77→
    78→function getLetterToggle(page: Page) {
    79→  return page.locator('button.type-btn').filter({ hasText: 'Letter' });
    80→}
    81→
    82→async function hasActiveClass(button: ReturnType<typeof getResumeToggle>): Promise<boolean> {
    83→  return button.evaluate(el => el.classList.contains('type-btn--active'));
    84→}
    85→
    86→// ─── FRG-217 Tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    87→
    88→// Test 1: Empty state shows sample buttons
    89→test('FRG-217 / 1. Empty state shows "Try Resume Sample" and "Try Letter Sample" buttons', async ({ page }) => {
    90→  await gotoAndWait(page);
    91→
    92→  // The right panel should show the empty state with sample buttons
    93→  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).toBeVisible();
    94→
    95→  const resumeSampleBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /try resume sample/i });
    96→  const letterSampleBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /try letter sample/i });
    97→
    98→  await expect(resumeSampleBtn, '"Try Resume Sample" button should be visible in empty state').toBeVisible();
    99→  await expect(letterSampleBtn, '"Try Letter Sample" button should be visible in empty state').toBeVisible();
   100→
   101→  // Both buttons should be enabled in the idle state
   102→  await expect(resumeSampleBtn).toBeEnabled();
   103→  await expect(letterSampleBtn).toBeEnabled();
   104→});
   105→
   106→// Test 2: Action bar shows sample links
   107→test('FRG-217 / 2. Action bar shows "Try a sample: Resume · Letter" links', async ({ page }) => {
   108→  await gotoAndWait(page);
   109→
   110→  // "Try a sample:" label in action bar (use class selector to avoid matching the empty-state hint)
   111→  await expect(page.locator('span.sample-label')).toBeVisible();
   112→
   113→  // Resume and Letter sample links in action bar
   114→  const resumeLink = page.locator('button.sample-link').filter({ hasText: /^Resume$/ });
   115→  const letterLink = page.locator('button.sample-link').filter({ hasText: /^Letter$/ });
   116→  const sepText = page.locator('.sample-sep');
   117→
   118→  await expect(resumeLink, '"Resume" sample link should be visible in action bar').toBeVisible();
   119→  await expect(letterLink, '"Letter" sample link should be visible in action bar').toBeVisible();
   120→  await expect(sepText, 'Separator "·" should be visible between sample links').toBeVisible();
   121→});
   122→
   123→// Test 3: Resume sample loads, populates textarea, switches doc type, triggers format
   124→test('FRG-217 / 3. Resume sample loads text, sets doc type to Resume, sends docType "resume" to API', async ({ page }) => {
   125→  let capturedBody: Record<string, unknown> | null = null;
   126→
   127→  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {
   128→    capturedBody = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');
   129→    await route.fulfill({
   130→      status: 200,
   131→      contentType: 'application/json',
   132→      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'resume' }),
   133→    });
   134→  });
   135→
   136→  await gotoAndWait(page);
   137→
   138→  // Click "Try Resume Sample" in the empty state panel
   139→  const resumeSampleBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /try resume sample/i });
   140→  await expect(resumeSampleBtn).toBeVisible();
   141→  await resumeSampleBtn.click();
   142→
   143→  // Textarea should now contain the sample resume text (Alex Morgan)
   144→  const textarea = page.locator('textarea');
   145→  await expect(async () => {
   146→    const value = await textarea.inputValue();
   147→    expect(value, 'Textarea should contain sample resume text').toContain('Alex Morgan');
   148→  }).toPass({ timeout: 5_000 });
   149→
   150→  // Doc type selector should switch to Resume (already default, but verify)
   151→  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);
   152→  expect(
   153→    await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn),
   154→    'Resume toggle should be active after loading resume sample',
   155→  ).toBe(true);
   156→
   157→  // Wait for formatting to complete (mocked)
   158→  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({
   159→    timeout: 15_000,
   160→  });
   161→  await expect(page.getByText('Preparing preview…')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 });
   162→
   163→  // API request body should contain docType: "resume"
   164→  expect(capturedBody, 'API request should have been made').not.toBeNull();
   165→  expect(capturedBody!.docType, 'Request body should contain docType: "resume"').toBe('resume');
   166→
   167→  // Formatted preview should show resume content (from mock XML)
   168→  await expect(page.getByText('Alex Morgan', { exact: false })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 });
   169→});
   170→
   171→// Test 4: Letter sample loads, populates textarea, switches doc type, triggers format
   172→test('FRG-217 / 4. Letter sample loads text, sets doc type to Letter, sends docType "letter" to API', async ({ page }) => {
   173→  let capturedBody: Record<string, unknown> | null = null;
   174→
   175→  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {
   176→    capturedBody = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');
   177→    await route.fulfill({
   178→      status: 200,
   179→      contentType: 'application/json',
   180→      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_LETTER_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'cover-letter' }),
   181→    });
   182→  });
   183→
   184→  await gotoAndWait(page);
   185→
   186→  // Click "Try Letter Sample" in the empty state panel
   187→  const letterSampleBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /try letter sample/i });
   188→  await expect(letterSampleBtn).toBeVisible();
   189→  await letterSampleBtn.click();
   190→
   191→  // Textarea should now contain the sample letter text (Jane Park)
   192→  const textarea = page.locator('textarea');
   193→  await expect(async () => {
   194→    const value = await textarea.inputValue();
   195→    expect(value, 'Textarea should contain sample letter text').toContain('Jane Park');
   196→  }).toPass({ timeout: 5_000 });
   197→
   198→  // Doc type selector should switch to Letter
   199→  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);
   200→  await expect(async () => {
   201→    expect(
   202→      await hasActiveClass(letterBtn),
   203→      'Letter toggle should be active after loading letter sample',
   204→    ).toBe(true);
   205→  }).toPass({ timeout: 3_000 });
   206→
   207→  // Wait for formatting to complete (mocked)
   208→  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({
   209→    timeout: 15_000,
   210→  });
   211→  await expect(page.getByText('Preparing preview…')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 });
   212→
   213→  // API request body should contain docType: "letter"
   214→  expect(capturedBody, 'API request should have been made').not.toBeNull();
   215→  expect(capturedBody!.docType, 'Request body should contain docType: "letter"').toBe('letter');
   216→
   217→  // Formatted preview should show letter content (from mock XML)
   218→  const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();
   219→  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain sender name').toMatch(/Jane Park/);
   220→  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain recipient company').toMatch(/Acme Technology/);
   221→});
   222→
   223→// Test 5: Buttons and links are disabled during formatting
   224→test('FRG-217 / 5. Sample links are disabled while formatting is in progress', async ({ page }) => {
   225→  // Intercept and delay the API for 10 seconds — enough time to verify the disabled state
   226→  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {
   227→    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 10_000));
   228→    await route.fulfill({
   229→      status: 200,
   230→      contentType: 'application/json',
   231→      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'resume' }),
   232→    });
   233→  });
   234→
   235→  await gotoAndWait(page);
   236→
   237→  // Use the action bar Resume sample link to trigger formatting
   238→  const resumeLink = page.locator('button.sample-link').filter({ hasText: /^Resume$/ });
   239→  await expect(resumeLink).toBeVisible();
   240→  await resumeLink.click();
   241→
   242→  // While the API request is in flight, sample links in action bar should be disabled
   243→  const letterLink = page.locator('button.sample-link').filter({ hasText: /^Letter$/ });
   244→
   245→  await expect(resumeLink, 'Resume sample link should be disabled during formatting').toBeDisabled({
   246→    timeout: 5_000,
   247→  });
   248→  await expect(letterLink, 'Letter sample link should be disabled during formatting').toBeDisabled({
   249→    timeout: 5_000,
   250→  });
   251→});
   252→
   253→// Test 6: Sample files exist and are non-empty
   254→test('FRG-217 / 6a. /samples/resume-sample.txt returns 200 and non-empty content', async ({ page }) => {
   255→  const response = await page.request.get(`${BASE_URL}/samples/resume-sample.txt`);
   256→
   257→  expect(response.status(), '/samples/resume-sample.txt should return HTTP 200').toBe(200);
   258→
   259→  const body = await response.text();
   260→  expect(body.trim().length, 'resume-sample.txt should have non-empty content').toBeGreaterThan(50);
   261→
   262→  // Should contain the expected sample person's name
   263→  expect(body, 'resume-sample.txt should contain "Alex Morgan"').toContain('Alex Morgan');
   264→});
   265→
   266→test('FRG-217 / 6b. /samples/letter-sample.txt returns 200 and non-empty content', async ({ page }) => {
   267→  const response = await page.request.get(`${BASE_URL}/samples/letter-sample.txt`);
   268→
   269→  expect(response.status(), '/samples/letter-sample.txt should return HTTP 200').toBe(200);
   270→
   271→  const body = await response.text();
   272→  expect(body.trim().length, 'letter-sample.txt should have non-empty content').toBeGreaterThan(50);
   273→
   274→  // Should contain the expected sample person's name
   275→  expect(body, 'letter-sample.txt should contain "Jane Park"').toContain('Jane Park');
   276→});
   277→
   278→// Test 7: Action bar Resume link also triggers correct behavior
   279→test('FRG-217 / 7. Action bar Resume link loads resume sample and sends docType "resume"', async ({ page }) => {
   280→  let capturedBody: Record<string, unknown> | null = null;
   281→
   282→  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {
   283→    capturedBody = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');
   284→    await route.fulfill({
   285→      status: 200,
   286→      contentType: 'application/json',
   287→      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'resume' }),
   288→    });
   289→  });
   290→
   291→  await gotoAndWait(page);
   292→
   293→  // Use action bar link instead of empty-state button
   294→  const resumeLink = page.locator('button.sample-link').filter({ hasText: /^Resume$/ });
   295→  await expect(resumeLink).toBeVisible();
   296→  await resumeLink.click();
   297→
   298→  // Textarea should be populated with Alex Morgan content
   299→  const textarea = page.locator('textarea');
   300→  await expect(async () => {
   301→    const value = await textarea.inputValue();
   302→    expect(value).toContain('Alex Morgan');
   303→  }).toPass({ timeout: 5_000 });
   304→
   305→  // Wait for mock response
   306→  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({
   307→    timeout: 15_000,
   308→  });
   309→  await expect(page.getByText('Preparing preview…')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 });
   310→
   311→  // Verify correct docType in API request
   312→  expect(capturedBody!.docType, 'Action bar Resume link should send docType: "resume"').toBe('resume');
   313→});
   314→
   315→// Test 8: Action bar Letter link also triggers correct behavior
   316→test('FRG-217 / 8. Action bar Letter link loads letter sample and sends docType "letter"', async ({ page }) => {
   317→  let capturedBody: Record<string, unknown> | null = null;
   318→
   319→  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {
   320→    capturedBody = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');
   321→    await route.fulfill({
   322→      status: 200,
   323→      contentType: 'application/json',
   324→      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_LETTER_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'cover-letter' }),
   325→    });
   326→  });
   327→
   328→  await gotoAndWait(page);
   329→
   330→  // Use action bar Letter link
   331→  const letterLink = page.locator('button.sample-link').filter({ hasText: /^Letter$/ });
   332→  await expect(letterLink).toBeVisible();
   333→  await letterLink.click();
   334→
   335→  // Textarea should be populated with Jane Park content
   336→  const textarea = page.locator('textarea');
   337→  await expect(async () => {
   338→    const value = await textarea.inputValue();
   339→    expect(value).toContain('Jane Park');
   340→  }).toPass({ timeout: 5_000 });
   341→
   342→  // Wait for mock response
   343→  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({
   344→    timeout: 15_000,
   345→  });
   346→  await expect(page.getByText('Preparing preview…')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 });
   347→
   348→  // Verify correct docType in API request
   349→  expect(capturedBody!.docType, 'Action bar Letter link should send docType: "letter"').toBe('letter');
   350→});

    "content": "     1\u2192import { test, expect, Page } from '@playwright/test';\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192const BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:3002';\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Mock XML responses \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192const MOCK_RESUME_XML = `<document type=\"resume\">\n     8\u2192  <header>\n     9\u2192    <name>Alex Morgan</name>\n    10\u2192    <contact>\n    11\u2192      <email>alex.morgan@email.com</email>\n    12\u2192      <phone>(416) 555-0182</phone>\n    13\u2192      <location>Toronto, ON</location>\n    14\u2192    </contact>\n    15\u2192  </header>\n    16\u2192  <summary>Senior Software Engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.</summary>\n    17\u2192  <experience>\n    18\u2192    <role>\n    19\u2192      <title>Senior Software Engineer</title>\n    20\u2192      <company>Shopify</company>\n    21\u2192      <period>March 2021 \u2013 Present</period>\n    22\u2192      <bullets>\n    23\u2192        <bullet>Led migration of core checkout service from monolith to microservices</bullet>\n    24\u2192      </bullets>\n    25\u2192    </role>\n    26\u2192  </experience>\n    27\u2192  <education>\n    28\u2192    <degree>Bachelor of Science, Computer Science</degree>\n    29\u2192    <school>University of Waterloo</school>\n    30\u2192    <year>2016</year>\n    31\u2192  </education>\n    32\u2192  <skills>JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, React, Node.js, PostgreSQL, AWS</skills>\n    33\u2192</document>`;\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192const MOCK_LETTER_XML = `<document type=\"cover-letter\">\n    36\u2192  <header>\n    37\u2192    <name>Jane Park</name>\n    38\u2192    <contact>\n    39\u2192      <email>jane.park@email.com</email>\n    40\u2192      <location>Vancouver, BC</location>\n    41\u2192    </contact>\n    42\u2192  </header>\n    43\u2192  <date>March 9, 2026</date>\n    44\u2192  <recipient>\n    45\u2192    <name>Hiring Manager</name>\n    46\u2192    <company>Acme Technology</company>\n    47\u2192    <address>San Francisco, CA</address>\n    48\u2192  </recipient>\n    49\u2192  <greeting>Dear Hiring Manager,</greeting>\n    50\u2192  <body>\n    51\u2192    <paragraph>I am writing to apply for the Product Designer position at Acme Technology.</paragraph>\n    52\u2192    <paragraph>Thank you for your time and consideration.</paragraph>\n    53\u2192  </body>\n    54\u2192  <closing>Sincerely,</closing>\n    55\u2192  <signature>Jane Park</signature>\n    56\u2192</document>`;\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192/**\n    61\u2192 * Navigate to BASE_URL and wait for full hydration (including Nuxt 3 client-side\n    62\u2192 * mounting) before the test body begins.\n    63\u2192 */\n    64\u2192async function gotoAndWait(page: Page): Promise<void> {\n    65\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n    66\u2192  await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle');\n    67\u2192  await page.locator('textarea').waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 15_000 });\n    68\u2192}\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192/**\n    71\u2192 * Scope type-toggle buttons to the .type-selector container to avoid matching\n    72\u2192 * the \"Format My Resume\" / \"Format My Letter\" UButton.\n    73\u2192 */\n    74\u2192function getResumeToggle(page: Page) {\n    75\u2192  return page.locator('button.type-btn').filter({ hasText: 'Resume' });\n    76\u2192}\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192function getLetterToggle(page: Page) {\n    79\u2192  return page.locator('button.type-btn').filter({ hasText: 'Letter' });\n    80\u2192}\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192async function hasActiveClass(button: ReturnType<typeof getResumeToggle>): Promise<boolean> {\n    83\u2192  return button.evaluate(el => el.classList.contains('type-btn--active'));\n    84\u2192}\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 FRG-217 Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192// Test 1: Empty state shows sample buttons\n    89\u2192test('FRG-217 / 1. Action bar shows \"Try a sample: Resume \u00b7 Letter\" links', async ({ page }) => {\n   108\u2192  await gotoAndWait(page);\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192  // \"Try a sample:\" label in action bar (use class selector to avoid matching the empty-state hint)\n   111\u2192  await expect(page.locator('span.sample-label')).toBeVisible();\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192  // Resume and Letter sample links in action bar\n   114\u2192  const resumeLink = page.locator('button.sample-link').filter({ hasText: /^Resume$/ });\n   115\u2192  const letterLink = page.locator('button.sample-link').filter({ hasText: /^Letter$/ });\n   116\u2192  const sepText = page.locator('.sample-sep');\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192  await expect(resumeLink, '\"Resume\" sample link should be visible in action bar').toBeVisible();\n   119\u2192  await expect(letterLink, '\"Letter\" sample link should be visible in action bar').toBeVisible();\n   120\u2192  await expect(sepText, 'Separator \"\u00b7\" should be visible between sample links').toBeVisible();\n   121\u2192});\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192// Test 3: Resume sample loads, populates textarea, switches doc type, triggers format\n   124\u2192test('FRG-217 / 3. page.request.get(`${BASE_URL}/samples/resume-sample.txt`);\n   256\u2192\n   257\u2192  expect(response.status(), '/samples/resume-sample.txt should return HTTP 200').toBe(200);\n   258\u2192\n   259\u2192  const body = await response.text();\n   260\u2192  expect(body.trim().length, 'resume-sample.txt should have non-empty content').toBeGreaterThan(50);\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192  // Should contain the expected sample person's name\n   263\u2192  expect(body, 'resume-sample.txt should contain \"Alex Morgan\"').toContain('Alex Morgan');\n   264\u2192});\n   265\u2192\n   266\u2192test('FRG-217 / 6b. /samples/letter-sample.txt returns 200 and non-empty content', async ({ page }) => {\n   267\u2192  const response = await page.request.get(`${BASE_URL}/samples/letter-sample.txt`);\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192  expect(response.status(), '/samples/letter-sample.txt should return HTTP 200').toBe(200);\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192  const body = await response.text();\n   272\u2192  expect(body.trim().length, 'letter-sample.txt should have non-empty content').toBeGreaterThan(50);\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192  // Should contain the expected sample person's name\n   275\u2192  expect(body, 'letter-sample.txt should contain \"Jane Park\"').toContain('Jane Park');\n   276\u2192});\n   277\u2192\n   278\u2192// Test 7: Action bar Resume link also triggers correct behavior\n   279\u2192test('FRG-217 / 7. Action bar Resume link loads resume sample and sends docType \"resume\"', async ({ page }) => {\n   280\u2192  let capturedBody: Record<string, unknown> | null = null;\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n   283\u2192    capturedBody = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');\n   284\u2192    await route.fulfill({\n   285\u2192      status: 200,\n   286\u2192      contentType: 'application/json',\n   287\u2192      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'resume' }),\n   288\u2192    });\n   289\u2192  });\n   290\u2192\n   291\u2192  await gotoAndWait(page);\n   292\u2192\n   293\u2192  // Use action bar link instead of empty-state button\n   294\u2192  const resumeLink = page.locator('button.sample-link').filter({ hasText: /^Resume$/ });\n   295\u2192  await expect(resumeLink).toBeVisible();\n   296\u2192  await resumeLink.click();\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192  // Textarea should be populated with Alex Morgan content\n   299\u2192  const textarea = page.locator('textarea');\n   300\u2192  await expect(async () => {\n   301\u2192    const value = await textarea.inputValue();\n   302\u2192    expect(value).toContain('Alex Morgan');\n   303\u2192  }).toPass({ timeout: 5_000 });\n   304\u2192\n   305\u2192  // Wait for mock response\n   306\u2192  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({\n   307\u2192    timeout: 15_000,\n   308\u2192  });\n   309\u2192  await expect(page.getByText('Preparing preview\u2026')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 });\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192  // Verify correct docType in API request\n   312\u2192  expect(capturedBody!.docType, 'Action bar Resume link should send docType: \"resume\"').toBe('resume');\n   313\u2192});\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192// Test 8: Action bar Letter link also triggers correct behavior\n   316\u2192test('FRG-217 / 8. Action bar Letter link loads letter sample and sends docType \"letter\"', async ({ page }) => {\n   317\u2192  let capturedBody: Record<string, unknown> | null = null;\n   318\u2192\n   319\u2192  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n   320\u2192    capturedBody = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');\n   321\u2192    await route.fulfill({\n   322\u2192      status: 200,\n   323\u2192      contentType: 'application/json',\n   324\u2192      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_LETTER_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'cover-letter' }),\n   325\u2192    });\n   326\u2192  });\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192  await gotoAndWait(page);\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192  // Use action bar Letter link\n   331\u2192  const letterLink = page.locator('button.sample-link').filter({ hasText: /^Letter$/ });\n   332\u2192  await expect(letterLink).toBeVisible();\n   333\u2192  await letterLink.click();\n   334\u2192\n   335\u2192  // Textarea should be populated with Jane Park content\n   336\u2192  const textarea = page.locator('textarea');\n   337\u2192  await expect(async () => {\n   338\u2192    const value = await textarea.inputValue();\n   339\u2192    expect(value).toContain('Jane Park');\n   340\u2192  }).toPass({ timeout: 5_000 });\n   341\u2192\n   342\u2192  // Wait for mock response\n   343\u2192  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({\n   344\u2192    timeout: 15_000,\n   345\u2192  });\n   346\u2192  await expect(page.getByText('Preparing preview\u2026')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 });\n   347\u2192\n   348\u2192  // Verify correct docType in API request\n   349\u2192  expect(capturedBody!.docType, 'Action bar Letter link should send docType: \"letter\"').toBe('letter');\n   350\u2192});\n   351\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.      1→import { test, expect, Page } from '@playwright/test';
     2→
     3→const BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:3000';
     4→
     5→// ─── Sample content ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     6→
     7→const RESUME_TEXT = `Alex Rivera
     8→alex.rivera@email.com · (555) 123-4567 · San Francisco, CA
     9→
    10→SUMMARY
    11→Full-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.
    12→
    13→EXPERIENCE
    14→Senior Software Engineer — Acme Corp (2021–Present)
    15→• Led development of a microservices architecture serving 2M+ daily active users
    16→• Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and query optimization
    17→
    18→EDUCATION
    19→B.S. Computer Science — University of California, Berkeley (2019)
    20→
    21→SKILLS
    22→JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Docker, AWS`.trim();
    23→
    24→const LETTER_TEXT = `Alex Rivera
    25→alex.rivera@email.com · San Francisco, CA
    26→
    27→March 4, 2026
    28→
    29→Hiring Manager
    30→TechVision Inc.
    31→123 Innovation Drive
    32→San Francisco, CA 94102
    33→
    34→Dear Hiring Manager,
    35→
    36→I am writing to express my strong interest in the Senior Software Engineer position at TechVision Inc.
    37→With seven years of experience building high-performance web applications, I am confident I would
    38→make a valuable addition to your team.
    39→
    40→Thank you for considering my application.
    41→
    42→Sincerely,
    43→Alex Rivera`.trim();
    44→
    45→// ─── Mock XML responses ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    46→
    47→const MOCK_RESUME_XML = `<document type="resume">
    48→  <header>
    49→    <name>Alex Rivera</name>
    50→    <contact>
    51→      <email>alex.rivera@email.com</email>
    52→      <phone>(555) 123-4567</phone>
    53→      <location>San Francisco, CA</location>
    54→    </contact>
    55→  </header>
    56→  <summary>Full-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.</summary>
    57→  <experience>
    58→    <role>
    59→      <title>Senior Software Engineer</title>
    60→      <company>Acme Corp</company>
    61→      <period>2021–Present</period>
    62→      <bullets>
    63→        <bullet>Led development of a microservices architecture serving 2M+ daily active users</bullet>
    64→        <bullet>Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and query optimization</bullet>
    65→      </bullets>
    66→    </role>
    67→  </experience>
    68→  <education>
    69→    <degree>B.S. Computer Science</degree>
    70→    <school>University of California, Berkeley</school>
    71→    <year>2019</year>
    72→  </education>
    73→  <skills>JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Docker, AWS</skills>
    74→</document>`;
    75→
    76→const MOCK_LETTER_XML = `<document type="cover-letter">
    77→  <header>
    78→    <name>Alex Rivera</name>
    79→    <contact>
    80→      <email>alex.rivera@email.com</email>
    81→      <location>San Francisco, CA</location>
    82→    </contact>
    83→  </header>
    84→  <date>March 4, 2026</date>
    85→  <recipient>
    86→    <name>Hiring Manager</name>
    87→    <company>TechVision Inc.</company>
    88→    <address>San Francisco, CA 94102</address>
    89→  </recipient>
    90→  <greeting>Dear Hiring Manager,</greeting>
    91→  <body>
    92→    <paragraph>I am writing to express my strong interest in the Senior Software Engineer position at TechVision Inc. With seven years of experience building high-performance web applications, I am confident I would make a valuable addition to your team.</paragraph>
    93→    <paragraph>Thank you for considering my application.</paragraph>
    94→  </body>
    95→  <closing>Sincerely,</closing>
    96→  <signature>Alex Rivera</signature>
    97→</document>`;
    98→
    99→// ─── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   100→
   101→/**
   102→ * Navigate to BASE_URL and wait for full hydration (including Nuxt 3 client-side
   103→ * mounting) before the test body begins.
   104→ */
   105→async function gotoAndWait(page: Page): Promise<void> {
   106→  await page.goto(BASE_URL);
   107→  await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle');
   108→  // Ensure the textarea is visible — confirms Vue has mounted
   109→  await page.locator('textarea').waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 15_000 });
   110→}
   111→
   112→/**
   113→ * Reliably fill a textarea and wait for Vue's reactive model to update.
   114→ * Uses click + fill + value verification to avoid intermittent fill() issues.
   115→ */
   116→async function fillTextarea(page: Page, text: string): Promise<void> {
   117→  const textarea = page.locator('textarea');
   118→  await textarea.click();
   119→  await textarea.fill(text);
   120→  await page.waitForTimeout(300);
   121→  // Verify the value was actually set; retry once if needed
   122→  const value = await textarea.inputValue();
   123→  if (value.length < 10 && text.length > 10) {
   124→    await textarea.click();
   125→    await textarea.fill(text);
   126→    await page.waitForTimeout(500);
   127→  }
   128→}
   129→
   130→/**
   131→ * Scope type-toggle buttons to the .type-selector container to avoid matching
   132→ * the "Format My Resume" / "Format My Letter" UButton, which also contains
   133→ * the words "Resume" and "Letter" in its accessible name.
   134→ */
   135→function getResumeToggle(page: Page) {
   136→  return page.locator('button.type-btn').filter({ hasText: 'Resume' });
   137→}
   138→
   139→function getLetterToggle(page: Page) {
   140→  return page.locator('button.type-btn').filter({ hasText: 'Letter' });
   141→}
   142→
   143→async function hasActiveClass(button: ReturnType<typeof getResumeToggle>): Promise<boolean> {
   144→  return button.evaluate(el => el.classList.contains('type-btn--active'));
   145→}
   146→
   147→// ─── FRG-216 Tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   148→
   149→// Test 1: Doc type selector renders
   150→test('FRG-216 / 1. Doc type selector renders with Resume active by default', async ({ page }) => {
   151→  await gotoAndWait(page);
   152→
   153→  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);
   154→  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);
   155→
   156→  // Both toggle buttons must exist
   157→  await expect(resumeBtn).toBeVisible();
   158→  await expect(letterBtn).toBeVisible();
   159→
   160→  // Resume is active by default
   161→  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn);
   162→  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should have active styling by default').toBe(true);
   163→
   164→  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(letterBtn);
   165→  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should NOT be active by default').toBe(false);
   166→
   167→  // Format button label reflects Resume selection
   168→  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).toBeVisible();
   169→});
   170→
   171→// Test 2: Toggle switches to Letter
   172→test('FRG-216 / 2. Toggle switches to Letter — button and label update', async ({ page }) => {
   173→  await gotoAndWait(page);
   174→
   175→  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);
   176→  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);
   177→
   178→  // Click Letter toggle
   179→  await letterBtn.click();
   180→  await page.waitForTimeout(300); // allow Vue reactivity
   181→
   182→  // Letter should become active, Resume should not
   183→  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(letterBtn);
   184→  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should have active styling after click').toBe(true);
   185→
   186→  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn);
   187→  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should lose active styling').toBe(false);
   188→
   189→  // Format button label must change to Letter
   190→  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });
   191→  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).not.toBeVisible();
   192→});
   193→
   194→// Test 3: Toggle switches back to Resume
   195→test('FRG-216 / 3. Toggle switches back to Resume', async ({ page }) => {
   196→  await gotoAndWait(page);
   197→
   198→  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);
   199→  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);
   200→
   201→  // Switch to Letter then back to Resume
   202→  await letterBtn.click();
   203→  await page.waitForTimeout(300);
   204→  await resumeBtn.click();
   205→  await page.waitForTimeout(300);
   206→
   207→  // Resume should be active again
   208→  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn);
   209→  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should be active after switching back').toBe(true);
   210→
   211→  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(letterBtn);
   212→  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should be inactive after switching back').toBe(false);
   213→
   214→  // Format button label must revert
   215→  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });
   216→  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).not.toBeVisible();
   217→});
   218→
   219→// Test 4: Type toggle buttons disabled during formatting
   220→test('FRG-216 / 4. Type toggle buttons are disabled while formatting is in progress', async ({ page }) => {
   221→  // Intercept and delay the API for 10 seconds — enough time to verify the disabled state
   222→  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {
   223→    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 10_000));
   224→    await route.fulfill({
   225→      status: 200,
   226→      contentType: 'application/json',
   227→      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'resume' }),
   228→    });
   229→  });
   230→
   231→  await gotoAndWait(page);
   232→  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);
   233→
   234→  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });
   235→  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });
   236→  await formatBtn.click();
   237→
   238→  // Both type-toggle buttons must be disabled while API request is in flight
   239→  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);
   240→  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);
   241→
   242→  await expect(resumeBtn, 'Resume toggle should be disabled during formatting').toBeDisabled({ timeout: 5_000 });
   243→  await expect(letterBtn, 'Letter toggle should be disabled during formatting').toBeDisabled({ timeout: 5_000 });
   244→});
   245→
   246→// Test 5: Resume formatting sends correct docType and shows resume XML elements
   247→test('FRG-216 / 5. Resume formatting — correct docType sent, resume XML in preview', async ({ page }) => {
   248→  let capturedDocType: string | null = null;
   249→
   250→  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {
   251→    const body = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');
   252→    capturedDocType = body.docType;
   253→    await route.fulfill({
   254→      status: 200,
   255→      contentType: 'application/json',
   256→      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.02, documentType: 'resume' }),
   257→    });
   258→  });
   259→
   260→  await gotoAndWait(page);
   261→
   262→  // Resume is selected by default
   263→  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);
   264→  expect(await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn), 'Resume toggle should be active by default').toBe(true);
   265→
   266→  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);
   267→  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });
   268→  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });
   269→  await formatBtn.click();
   270→
   271→  // Wait for preview to replace the placeholder
   272→  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 });
   273→
   274→  // docType sent in request must be 'resume'
   275→  expect(capturedDocType, 'Request body should contain docType: "resume"').toBe('resume');
   276→
   277→  // Preview should render content from the mocked resume XML
   278→  await expect(page.getByText('Alex Rivera', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();
   279→  await expect(page.getByText('Acme Corp', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();
   280→});
   281→
   282→// Test 6: Letter formatting sends correct docType and shows letter XML elements
   283→test('FRG-216 / 6. Letter formatting — correct docType sent, letter XML in preview', async ({ page }) => {
   284→  let capturedDocType: string | null = null;
   285→
   286→  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {
   287→    const body = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');
   288→    capturedDocType = body.docType;
   289→    await route.fulfill({
   290→      status: 200,
   291→      contentType: 'application/json',
   292→      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_LETTER_XML, divergence: 0.02, documentType: 'cover-letter' }),
   293→    });
   294→  });
   295→
   296→  await gotoAndWait(page);
   297→
   298→  // Switch to Letter
   299→  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);
   300→  await letterBtn.click();
   301→  await page.waitForTimeout(300);
   302→  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });
   303→
   304→  await fillTextarea(page, LETTER_TEXT);
   305→  await page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i }).click();
   306→
   307→  // Wait for the initial placeholder to go away, then for the async DOMPurify
   308→  // sanitization step ("Preparing preview…") to also complete.
   309→  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 });
   310→  await expect(page.getByText('Preparing preview…')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 });
   311→
   312→  // docType sent in request must be 'letter'
   313→  expect(capturedDocType, 'Request body should contain docType: "letter"').toBe('letter');
   314→
   315→  // Verify letter-specific content in the rendered preview.
   316→  // Some strings appear in multiple XML nodes (name/signature, company/paragraph)
   317→  // so we assert on the full body text rather than individual locators.
   318→  const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();
   319→  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain sender name').toMatch(/Alex Rivera/);
   320→  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain recipient company').toMatch(/TechVision/);
   321→  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain greeting').toMatch(/Dear Hiring Manager/i);
   322→  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain closing').toMatch(/Sincerely/i);
   323→});
   324→

    "tool_use_id": "toolu_014NgL4KJLYkkSCq5mWkNiLA",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192import { test, expect, Page } from '@playwright/test';\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192const BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:3000';\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Sample content \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192const RESUME_TEXT = `Alex Rivera\n     8\u2192alex.rivera@email.com \u00b7 (555) 123-4567 \u00b7 San Francisco, CA\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192SUMMARY\n    11\u2192Full-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192EXPERIENCE\n    14\u2192Senior Software Engineer \u2014 Acme Corp (2021\u2013Present)\n    15\u2192\u2022 Led development of a microservices architecture serving 2M+ daily active users\n    16\u2192\u2022 Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and query optimization\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192EDUCATION\n    19\u2192B.S. Computer Science \u2014 University of California, Berkeley (2019)\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192SKILLS\n    22\u2192JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Docker, AWS`.trim();\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192const LETTER_TEXT = `Alex Rivera\n    25\u2192alex.rivera@email.com \u00b7 San Francisco, CA\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192March 4, 2026\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192Hiring Manager\n    30\u2192TechVision Inc.\n    31\u2192123 Innovation Drive\n    32\u2192San Francisco, CA 94102\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192Dear Hiring Manager,\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192I am writing to express my strong interest in the Senior Software Engineer position at TechVision Inc.\n    37\u2192With seven years of experience building high-performance web applications, I am confident I would\n    38\u2192make a valuable addition to your team.\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192Thank you for considering my application.\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192Sincerely,\n    43\u2192Alex Rivera`.trim();\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Mock XML responses \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192const MOCK_RESUME_XML = `<document type=\"resume\">\n    48\u2192  <header>\n    49\u2192    <name>Alex Rivera</name>\n    50\u2192    <contact>\n    51\u2192      <email>alex.rivera@email.com</email>\n    52\u2192      <phone>(555) 123-4567</phone>\n    53\u2192      <location>San Francisco, CA</location>\n    54\u2192    </contact>\n    55\u2192  </header>\n    56\u2192  <summary>Full-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.</summary>\n    57\u2192  <experience>\n    58\u2192    <role>\n    59\u2192      <title>Senior Software Engineer</title>\n    60\u2192      <company>Acme Corp</company>\n    61\u2192      <period>2021\u2013Present</period>\n    62\u2192      <bullets>\n    63\u2192        <bullet>Led development of a microservices architecture serving 2M+ daily active users</bullet>\n    64\u2192        <bullet>Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and query optimization</bullet>\n    65\u2192      </bullets>\n    66\u2192    </role>\n    67\u2192  </experience>\n    68\u2192  <education>\n    69\u2192    <degree>B.S. Computer Science</degree>\n    70\u2192    <school>University of California, Berkeley</school>\n    71\u2192    <year>2019</year>\n    72\u2192  </education>\n    73\u2192  <skills>JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Docker, AWS</skills>\n    74\u2192</document>`;\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192const MOCK_LETTER_XML = `<document type=\"cover-letter\">\n    77\u2192  <header>\n    78\u2192    <name>Alex Rivera</name>\n    79\u2192    <contact>\n    80\u2192      <email>alex.rivera@email.com</email>\n    81\u2192      <location>San Francisco, CA</location>\n    82\u2192    </contact>\n    83\u2192  </header>\n    84\u2192  <date>March 4, 2026</date>\n    85\u2192  <recipient>\n    86\u2192    <name>Hiring Manager</name>\n    87\u2192    <company>TechVision Inc.</company>\n    88\u2192    <address>San Francisco, CA 94102</address>\n    89\u2192  </recipient>\n    90\u2192  <greeting>Dear Hiring Manager,</greeting>\n    91\u2192  <body>\n    92\u2192    <paragraph>I am writing to express my strong interest in the Senior Software Engineer position at TechVision Inc. With seven years of experience building high-performance web applications, I am confident I would make a valuable addition to your team.</paragraph>\n    93\u2192    <paragraph>Thank you for considering my application.</paragraph>\n    94\u2192  </body>\n    95\u2192  <closing>Sincerely,</closing>\n    96\u2192  <signature>Alex Rivera</signature>\n    97\u2192</document>`;\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192/**\n   102\u2192 * Navigate to BASE_URL and wait for full hydration (including Nuxt 3 client-side\n   103\u2192 * mounting) before the test body begins.\n   104\u2192 */\n   105\u2192async function gotoAndWait(page: Page): Promise<void> {\n   106\u2192  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n   107\u2192  await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle');\n   108\u2192  // Ensure the textarea is visible \u2014 confirms Vue has mounted\n   109\u2192  await page.locator('textarea').waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 15_000 });\n   110\u2192}\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192/**\n   113\u2192 * Reliably fill a textarea and wait for Vue's reactive model to update.\n   114\u2192 * Uses click + fill + value verification to avoid intermittent fill() issues.\n   115\u2192 */\n   116\u2192async function fillTextarea(page: Page, text: string): Promise<void> {\n   117\u2192  const textarea = page.locator('textarea');\n   118\u2192  await textarea.click();\n   119\u2192  await textarea.fill(text);\n   120\u2192  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n   121\u2192  // Verify the value was actually set; retry once if needed\n   122\u2192  const value = await textarea.inputValue();\n   123\u2192  if (value.length < 10 && text.length > 10) {\n   124\u2192    await textarea.click();\n   125\u2192    await textarea.fill(text);\n   126\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n   127\u2192  }\n   128\u2192}\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192/**\n   131\u2192 * Scope type-toggle buttons to the .type-selector container to avoid matching\n   132\u2192 * the \"Format My Resume\" / \"Format My Letter\" UButton, which also contains\n   133\u2192 * the words \"Resume\" and \"Letter\" in its accessible name.\n   134\u2192 */\n   135\u2192function getResumeToggle(page: Page) {\n   136\u2192  return page.locator('button.type-btn').filter({ hasText: 'Resume' });\n   137\u2192}\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192function getLetterToggle(page: Page) {\n   140\u2192  return page.locator('button.type-btn').filter({ hasText: 'Letter' });\n   141\u2192}\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192async function hasActiveClass(button: ReturnType<typeof getResumeToggle>): Promise<boolean> {\n   144\u2192  return button.evaluate(el => el.classList.contains('type-btn--active'));\n   145\u2192}\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500 FRG-216 Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192// Test 1: Doc type selector renders\n   150\u2192test('FRG-216 / 1. Doc type selector renders with Resume active by default', async ({ page }) => {\n   151\u2192  await gotoAndWait(page);\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);\n   154\u2192  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192  // Both toggle buttons must exist\n   157\u2192  await expect(resumeBtn).toBeVisible();\n   158\u2192  await expect(letterBtn).toBeVisible();\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192  // Resume is active by default\n   161\u2192  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn);\n   162\u2192  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should have active styling by default').toBe(true);\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(letterBtn);\n   165\u2192  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should NOT be active by default').toBe(false);\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192  // Format button label reflects Resume selection\n   168\u2192  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).toBeVisible();\n   169\u2192});\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192// Test 2: Toggle switches to Letter\n   172\u2192test('FRG-216 / 2. Toggle switches to Letter \u2014 button and label update', async ({ page }) => {\n   173\u2192  await gotoAndWait(page);\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);\n   176\u2192  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192  // Click Letter toggle\n   179\u2192  await letterBtn.click();\n   180\u2192  await page.waitForTimeout(300); // allow Vue reactivity\n   181\u2192\n   182\u2192  // Letter should become active, Resume should not\n   183\u2192  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(letterBtn);\n   184\u2192  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should have active styling after click').toBe(true);\n   185\u2192\n   186\u2192  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn);\n   187\u2192  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should lose active styling').toBe(false);\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192  // Format button label must change to Letter\n   190\u2192  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n   191\u2192  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).not.toBeVisible();\n   192\u2192});\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192// Test 3: Toggle switches back to Resume\n   195\u2192test('FRG-216 / 3. Toggle switches back to Resume', async ({ page }) => {\n   196\u2192  await gotoAndWait(page);\n   197\u2192\n   198\u2192  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);\n   199\u2192  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192  // Switch to Letter then back to Resume\n   202\u2192  await letterBtn.click();\n   203\u2192  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n   204\u2192  await resumeBtn.click();\n   205\u2192  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192  // Resume should be active again\n   208\u2192  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn);\n   209\u2192  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should be active after switching back').toBe(true);\n   210\u2192\n   211\u2192  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(letterBtn);\n   212\u2192  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should be inactive after switching back').toBe(false);\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192  // Format button label must revert\n   215\u2192  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n   216\u2192  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).not.toBeVisible();\n   217\u2192});\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192// Test 4: Type toggle buttons disabled during formatting\n   220\u2192test('FRG-216 / 4. Type toggle buttons are disabled while formatting is in progress', async ({ page }) => {\n   221\u2192  // Intercept and delay the API for 10 seconds \u2014 enough time to verify the disabled state\n   222\u2192  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n   223\u2192    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 10_000));\n   224\u2192    await route.fulfill({\n   225\u2192      status: 200,\n   226\u2192      contentType: 'application/json',\n   227\u2192      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'resume' }),\n   228\u2192    });\n   229\u2192  });\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192  await gotoAndWait(page);\n   232\u2192  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n   235\u2192  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n   236\u2192  await formatBtn.click();\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192  // Both type-toggle buttons must be disabled while API request is in flight\n   239\u2192  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);\n   240\u2192  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);\n   241\u2192\n   242\u2192  await expect(resumeBtn, 'Resume toggle should be disabled during formatting').toBeDisabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n   243\u2192  await expect(letterBtn, 'Letter toggle should be disabled during formatting').toBeDisabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n   244\u2192});\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192// Test 5: Resume formatting sends correct docType and shows resume XML elements\n   247\u2192test('FRG-216 / 5. Resume formatting \u2014 correct docType sent, resume XML in preview', async ({ page }) => {\n   248\u2192  let capturedDocType: string | null = null;\n   249\u2192\n   250\u2192  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n   251\u2192    const body = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');\n   252\u2192    capturedDocType = body.docType;\n   253\u2192    await route.fulfill({\n   254\u2192      status: 200,\n   255\u2192      contentType: 'application/json',\n   256\u2192      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.02, documentType: 'resume' }),\n   257\u2192    });\n   258\u2192  });\n   259\u2192\n   260\u2192  await gotoAndWait(page);\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192  // Resume is selected by default\n   263\u2192  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);\n   264\u2192  expect(await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn), 'Resume toggle should be active by default').toBe(true);\n   265\u2192\n   266\u2192  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n   267\u2192  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n   268\u2192  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n   269\u2192  await formatBtn.click();\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192  // Wait for preview to replace the placeholder\n   272\u2192  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 });\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192  // docType sent in request must be 'resume'\n   275\u2192  expect(capturedDocType, 'Request body should contain docType: \"resume\"').toBe('resume');\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192  // Preview should render content from the mocked resume XML\n   278\u2192  await expect(page.getByText('Alex Rivera', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n   279\u2192  await expect(page.getByText('Acme Corp', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n   280\u2192});\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192// Test 6: Letter formatting sends correct docType and shows letter XML elements\n   283\u2192test('FRG-216 / 6. Letter formatting \u2014 correct docType sent, letter XML in preview', async ({ page }) => {\n   284\u2192  let capturedDocType: string | null = null;\n   285\u2192\n   286\u2192  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n   287\u2192    const body = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');\n   288\u2192    capturedDocType = body.docType;\n   289\u2192    await route.fulfill({\n   290\u2192      status: 200,\n   291\u2192      contentType: 'application/json',\n   292\u2192      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_LETTER_XML, divergence: 0.02, documentType: 'cover-letter' }),\n   293\u2192    });\n   294\u2192  });\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192  await gotoAndWait(page);\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192  // Switch to Letter\n   299\u2192  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);\n   300\u2192  await letterBtn.click();\n   301\u2192  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n   302\u2192  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192  await fillTextarea(page, LETTER_TEXT);\n   305\u2192  await page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i }).click();\n   306\u2192\n   307\u2192  // Wait for the initial placeholder to go away, then for the async DOMPurify\n   308\u2192  // sanitization step (\"Preparing preview\u2026\") to also complete.\n   309\u2192  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 });\n   310\u2192  await expect(page.getByText('Preparing preview\u2026')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 });\n   311\u2192\n   312\u2192  // docType sent in request must be 'letter'\n   313\u2192  expect(capturedDocType, 'Request body should contain docType: \"letter\"').toBe('letter');\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192  // Verify letter-specific content in the rendered preview.\n   316\u2192  // Some strings appear in multiple XML nodes (name/signature, company/paragraph)\n   317\u2192  // so we assert on the full body text rather than individual locators.\n   318\u2192  const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();\n   319\u2192  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain sender name').toMatch(/Alex Rivera/);\n   320\u2192  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain recipient company').toMatch(/TechVision/);\n   321\u2192  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain greeting').toMatch(/Dear Hiring Manager/i);\n   322\u2192  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain closing').toMatch(/Sincerely/i);\n   323\u2192});\n   324\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #28
The URL is protected by Vercel SSO/authentication. The URL `https://ohmydoc-using-claude-c-git-6204d7-kimwongwwk-gmailcoms-projects.vercel.app` requires Vercel login to access (it's a preview deployment under a team with SSO protection). The existing tests in the repo use a different BASE_URL (the production `https://ohmydoc.vercel.app`). However, there's also the FRG-216 and FRG-217 tests that use `http://localhost:3000` and `http://localhost:3002` respectively (likely meant for local testing with mocks). Since I can't directly explore the SSO-protected preview URL, I'll need to write the tests using the BASE_URL environment variable and infer the UI structure from existing test patterns and common conventions. Some of the existing tests use API mocking while others hit the actual endpoints, so I'll follow that same approach. I need to create the test file in `tests/e2e/frg-215-edit-xml.spec.ts` since the e2e subdirectory doesn't exist yet, and the Playwright config will pick up subdirectories under tests. Now I'm planning out the test cases, starting with verifying the "Edit XML" button doesn't appear before formatting happens. visible **Test 2: "Edit XML" button appears after formatting** - Mock the API - Format a resume - Verify "Edit XML" button appears **Test 3: Click "Edit XML" shows XML textarea** - Mock the API - Format a resume - Click "Edit XML" - Verify XML textarea appears with monospace font - Verify it contains the XML content **Test 4: Editing XML updates preview in real-time** - Mock the API - Format a resume - Click "Edit XML" - Edit XML content (change a name) - Verify preview updates with the new content **Test 5: "← Back to text" returns to text input view** - Mock the API - Format a resume - Click "Edit XML" - Click "← Back to text" - Verify textarea with original text is shown **Test 6: Re-formatting with AI in XML mode re-runs the formatting and updates the preview after sending an API request** **Test 7: Full flow - Try a sample → format → edit XML** - Click sample button - Format - Click "Edit XML" - Verify XML content is shown Now I'm thinking about the selectors I'll need. Since I can't inspect the live app directly, I'll rely on flexible patterns using role-based selectors like `getByRole('button', { name: ... })` rather than class selectors, which should be more resilient. I'll use descriptive patterns for the "Edit XML" button and other interactive elements. For the BASE_URL, the deployed environment requires authentication, so I'll follow the existing pattern of using `process.env.BASE_URL` and note this constraint in the tests. For tests involving XML editing with real-time preview updates, I'll need to mock the API responses to ensure reliable assertions about the preview behavior. Writing the test file now... I need to configure the test file to use `BASE_URL` from environment variables with a fallback to the Vercel deployment URL, then check how the CI workflow runs these tests to ensure they'll work in that context.

USERMESSAGE #35
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→name: Playwright Tests
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  push:
     5→    branches: [main]
     6→  pull_request:
     7→    branches: [main]
     8→  schedule:
     9→    # Weekly smoke run every Monday at 08:00 UTC
    10→    - cron: '0 8 * * 1'
    11→
    12→jobs:
    13→  test:
    14→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    15→    timeout-minutes: 30
    16→
    17→    steps:
    18→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    19→
    20→      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
    21→        with:
    22→          node-version: 20
    23→          cache: npm
    24→
    25→      - name: Install dependencies
    26→        run: npm ci
    27→
    28→      - name: Install Chromium
    29→        run: npx playwright install chromium --with-deps
    30→
    31→      - name: Run Playwright tests
    32→        run: npx playwright test
    33→        env:
    34→          BASE_URL: ${{ vars.BASE_URL || 'https://ohmydoc.vercel.app' }}
    35→
    36→      - name: Upload HTML report
    37→        if: always()
    38→        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
    39→        with:
    40→          name: playwright-report
    41→          path: playwright-report/
    42→          retention-days: 30
    43→
    44→      - name: Upload JSON results
    45→        if: always()
    46→        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
    47→        with:
    48→          name: test-results-json
    49→          path: test-reports/results.json
    50→          retention-days: 30
    51→

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #36
Now I have a good understanding of the project structure. The tests use `BASE_URL` from env var. The deployed URL given in the task requires Vercel SSO auth. Since: 1. I can't browse the live URL to inspect the UI 2. The existing FRG-216 tests use API mocking patterns that don't require the live AI API 3. The FRG-217 tests point to `http://localhost:3002` (local dev server) by default For the FRG-215 tests, I'll follow the same pattern as FRG-216 (using mocks for most tests) so they can run reliably. For the test that uses the real AI API, I'll use `test.slow()` and the existing helpers. I need to figure out what selectors to use for the new elements. Since I can't inspect the live page directly, I'll base my selectors on the FRG-215 feature description and the role-based patterns already established in the codebase—looking for the "Edit XML" button, the XML textarea, the "Back to text" link, and the "Re-format with AI" button that appear in the XML editing mode. I'm noticing the test files use different localhost ports—FRG-217 runs on 3002 while FRG-216 uses 3000—but since the task requires running against the deployed URL, I'll set that as the default while allowing `process.env.BASE_URL` to override it. The deployed instance requires SSO, though the tests use API mocking which should work across any environment since the mocked routes intercept the API calls before they hit the actual backend. For FRG-215, I should set up API mocking like the other tests so they can run against the deployed feature branch URL without needing real API calls, and use the `BASE_URL` env variable to point to the correct deployment. Setting up the test file with environment-based configuration and planning the test structure with both mocked and live variants, then identifying the key selectors for the Edit XML feature. Writing the test file... Writing the test file... I'm defining the mock resume XML data with contact information, work experience, education, and skills sections to use in the test. Now I'm setting up a helper function that navigates to the base URL and waits for the page to load... Writing test utilities... Writing test helper functions... Now I'm setting up the mock for the API endpoint to return formatted XML with metadata, then navigating to the page, filling in the resume text, and waiting for the preview to render after clicking the format button. I've outlined five test cases that verify the "Edit XML" button's visibility before and after formatting, the ability to switch to XML mode, and that the generated XML displays correctly in the textarea. For the remaining tests, I need to handle real-time preview updates when editing the XML directly—I'll modify the name field in the textarea and verify the preview refreshes, then test the navigation buttons that switch between text and XML modes. The final test will follow the same mock routing pattern to test the full flow including the sample button. Writing helper functions... I'm reconsidering the approach—instead of trying to filter by text content, I should wait for the XML editor to appear and then grab the textarea that contains actual XML, checking its value directly rather than relying on visibility or text matching. I'm realizing the SSO protection on the deployed URL is a real blocker—the tests will fail at the initial navigation step if they hit the Vercel login page instead of the app. I need to figure out whether these tests should be skipped for SSO-protected environments or if there's a way to handle authentication in the test setup. Looking at the existing test patterns in FRG-216 and FRG-217, they're designed to run locally with API mocking, which suggests I should follow that same approach. I'll set up the tests to use a fallback to the production URL when `BASE_URL` isn't set, but I'll add a note that the feature branch preview won't work without handling the SSO authentication first. I'll set up the test file using `process.env.BASE_URL` with a fallback to the production URL, then run the tests against the accessible deployment to verify everything works, even if the specific feature isn't available yet on the SSO-protected preview. Writing the test file...
