Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/e2e/frg-215-edit-xml.spec.ts",
  "content": "import { test, expect, Page } from '@playwright/test';\n\n/**\n * FRG-215 \u2014 View / Edit XML\n *\n * Feature: after AI formatting an \"Edit XML\" button appears next to the Format\n * button.  Clicking it reveals the raw XML in an editable textarea (monospace).\n * Editing the XML updates the preview in real-time.  A \"\u2190 Back to text\" control\n * returns to the original text-input view.  A \"Re-format with AI\" button inside\n * XML mode clears XML, restores the text input, and re-runs AI formatting.\n *\n * Target deployment:\n *   https://ohmydoc-using-claude-c-git-6204d7-kimwongwwk-gmailcoms-projects.vercel.app\n *   (Set BASE_URL env var to override; falls back to production when that URL is\n *    behind Vercel SSO.)\n */\n\nconst BASE_URL =\n  process.env.BASE_URL ||\n  'https://ohmydoc-using-claude-c-git-6204d7-kimwongwwk-gmailcoms-projects.vercel.app';\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Mock XML \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nconst MOCK_RESUME_XML = `<document type=\"resume\">\n  <header>\n    <name>Alex Rivera</name>\n    <contact>\n      <email>alex.rivera@email.com</email>\n      <phone>(555) 123-4567</phone>\n      <location>San Francisco, CA</location>\n    </contact>\n  </header>\n  <summary>Full-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.</summary>\n  <experience>\n    <role>\n      <title>Senior Software Engineer</title>\n      <company>Acme Corp</company>\n      <period>2021\u2013Present</period>\n      <bullets>\n        <bullet>Led microservices migration serving 2M+ daily active users</bullet>\n        <bullet>Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and query optimisation</bullet>\n      </bullets>\n    </role>\n  </experience>\n  <education>\n    <degree>B.S. Computer Science</degree>\n    <school>University of California, Berkeley</school>\n    <year>2019</year>\n  </education>\n  <skills>JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Docker, AWS</skills>\n</document>`;\n\nconst RESUME_TEXT = `Alex Rivera\nalex.rivera@email.com \u00b7 (555) 123-4567 \u00b7 San Francisco, CA\n\nSUMMARY\nFull-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.\n\nEXPERIENCE\nSenior Software Engineer \u2014 Acme Corp (2021\u2013Present)\n\u2022 Led microservices migration serving 2M+ daily active users\n\u2022 Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and query optimisation\n\nEDUCATION\nB.S. Computer Science \u2014 University of California, Berkeley (2019)\n\nSKILLS\nJavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Docker, AWS`.trim();\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n/** Navigate to BASE_URL and wait for full Vue/Nuxt hydration. */\nasync function gotoAndWait(page: Page): Promise<void> {\n  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n  await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle');\n  await page.locator('textarea').first().waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 15_000 });\n}\n\n/** Reliably fill a textarea and wait for Vue reactivity. */\nasync function fillTextarea(page: Page, text: string): Promise<void> {\n  const textarea = page.locator('textarea').first();\n  await textarea.click();\n  await textarea.fill(text);\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n  const value = await textarea.inputValue();\n  if (value.length < 10 && text.length > 10) {\n    await textarea.click();\n    await textarea.fill(text);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n  }\n}\n\n/** Wait for the AI formatting placeholder to disappear (preview rendered). */\nasync function waitForPreview(page: Page): Promise<void> {\n  await expect(\n    page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.'),\n  ).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 });\n  // Dismiss any transient \"Preparing preview\u2026\" message\n  await page\n    .getByText(/preparing preview/i)\n    .waitFor({ state: 'hidden', timeout: 10_000 })\n    .catch(() => {/* element may never appear \u2014 that's fine */});\n}\n\n/** The \"Edit XML\" button that appears in the action bar after formatting. */\nfunction getEditXmlButton(page: Page) {\n  return page.getByRole('button', { name: /edit xml/i });\n}\n\n/** The \"\u2190 Back to text\" control (button or link) shown in XML mode. */\nfunction getBackToTextControl(page: Page) {\n  return page\n    .getByRole('button', { name: /back to text/i })\n    .or(page.getByRole('link', { name: /back to text/i }))\n    .or(page.getByText(/\u2190\\s*back to text/i));\n}\n\n/** The \"Re-format with AI\" button shown inside XML mode. */\nfunction getReformatButton(page: Page) {\n  return page.getByRole('button', { name: /re-format with ai/i });\n}\n\n/**\n * Find the textarea that contains raw XML (the XML editor, not the text input).\n * Waits up to 5 s for at least one textarea to hold XML content.\n */\nasync function getXmlTextarea(page: Page) {\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => {\n      const tas = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('textarea'));\n      return tas.some(ta => ta.value.trimStart().startsWith('<'));\n    },\n    undefined,\n    { timeout: 10_000 },\n  );\n  const all = await page.locator('textarea').all();\n  for (const ta of all) {\n    const val = await ta.inputValue();\n    if (val.trimStart().startsWith('<')) return ta;\n  }\n  // Fallback \u2014 should not normally be reached\n  return page.locator('textarea').first();\n}\n\n/**\n * Mock the /api/format endpoint, navigate, fill the textarea, click Format,\n * and wait for the preview.  Returns after the preview is rendered.\n */\nasync function formatWithMock(\n  page: Page,\n  mockXml: string = MOCK_RESUME_XML,\n): Promise<void> {\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: mockXml, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'resume' }),\n    });\n  });\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await formatBtn.click();\n  await waitForPreview(page);\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 FRG-215 Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n// \u2500\u2500 1. Pre-format state \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 1. \"Edit XML\" button is NOT visible before formatting', async ({ page }) => {\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n\n  // The button must not be present (or must be hidden) when no XML has been generated\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  const isVisible = await editXmlBtn.isVisible().catch(() => false);\n  expect(isVisible, '\"Edit XML\" button should not be visible before any formatting').toBe(false);\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 2. Post-format state \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 2. \"Edit XML\" button appears after formatting', async ({ page }) => {\n  await formatWithMock(page);\n\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn, '\"Edit XML\" button should be visible after formatting completes').toBeVisible({\n    timeout: 5_000,\n  });\n  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeEnabled();\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 3. Enter XML edit mode \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 3. Clicking \"Edit XML\" shows XML textarea and hides text input', async ({ page }) => {\n  await formatWithMock(page);\n\n  // Click the Edit XML button\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await editXmlBtn.click();\n\n  // An XML textarea must now be visible\n  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);\n  await expect(xmlTextarea, 'XML textarea should be visible after clicking Edit XML').toBeVisible({\n    timeout: 5_000,\n  });\n\n  // The XML textarea must contain the generated XML\n  const xmlContent = await xmlTextarea.inputValue();\n  expect(xmlContent.trim(), 'XML textarea should contain the generated XML').toMatch(/^<document/i);\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 4. XML content fidelity \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 4. XML textarea displays the generated XML with correct content', async ({ page }) => {\n  await formatWithMock(page);\n\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await editXmlBtn.click();\n\n  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);\n  const xmlContent = await xmlTextarea.inputValue();\n\n  // The XML must contain the name from the mock response\n  expect(xmlContent, 'XML should contain \"Alex Rivera\"').toContain('Alex Rivera');\n  // The XML must contain the employer from the mock response\n  expect(xmlContent, 'XML should contain \"Acme Corp\"').toContain('Acme Corp');\n  // Basic XML structure\n  expect(xmlContent, 'XML should have a closing </document> tag').toContain('</document>');\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 5. Monospace font \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 5. XML textarea uses a monospace font', async ({ page }) => {\n  await formatWithMock(page);\n\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await editXmlBtn.click();\n\n  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);\n\n  // Check computed font-family contains a monospace indicator\n  const fontFamily = await xmlTextarea.evaluate(\n    el => window.getComputedStyle(el).fontFamily.toLowerCase(),\n  );\n  const isMonospace =\n    fontFamily.includes('mono') ||\n    fontFamily.includes('courier') ||\n    fontFamily.includes('consolas') ||\n    fontFamily.includes('menlo') ||\n    fontFamily.includes('source code') ||\n    fontFamily.includes('fira code');\n\n  expect(isMonospace, `XML textarea font-family \"${fontFamily}\" should be monospace`).toBe(true);\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 6. Real-time preview update on XML edit \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 6. Editing the XML updates the preview in real-time', async ({ page }) => {\n  await formatWithMock(page);\n\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await editXmlBtn.click();\n\n  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);\n  const originalXml = await xmlTextarea.inputValue();\n\n  // Replace the name in the XML to a unique test value\n  const modifiedXml = originalXml.replace(/Alex Rivera/g, 'Taylor Playwright');\n  await xmlTextarea.fill(modifiedXml);\n  await page.waitForTimeout(500); // allow debounce / reactive update\n\n  // The preview pane should reflect the edited name\n  const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();\n  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should update with the edited name \"Taylor Playwright\"').toContain(\n    'Taylor Playwright',\n  );\n  expect(bodyText, 'Original name \"Alex Rivera\" should no longer appear in preview').not.toContain(\n    'Alex Rivera',\n  );\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 7. Back to text \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 7. \"\u2190 Back to text\" returns to the text input view', async ({ page }) => {\n  await formatWithMock(page);\n\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await editXmlBtn.click();\n\n  // Confirm XML mode is active\n  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);\n  await expect(xmlTextarea).toBeVisible();\n\n  // Click Back to text\n  const backLink = getBackToTextControl(page);\n  await expect(backLink, '\"\u2190 Back to text\" control should be visible in XML mode').toBeVisible({\n    timeout: 5_000,\n  });\n  await backLink.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n\n  // The main text textarea must become visible again\n  const mainTextarea = page.locator('textarea').first();\n  await expect(mainTextarea, 'Main text textarea should be visible after \"Back to text\"').toBeVisible({\n    timeout: 5_000,\n  });\n\n  // The original resume text should be present in the textarea\n  const textValue = await mainTextarea.inputValue();\n  expect(textValue.length, 'Text textarea should still contain the original input').toBeGreaterThan(50);\n\n  // The XML textarea should no longer be visible\n  const xmlAfter = await page.locator('textarea').all();\n  let xmlVisible = false;\n  for (const ta of xmlAfter) {\n    const val = await ta.inputValue();\n    if (val.trimStart().startsWith('<') && await ta.isVisible()) {\n      xmlVisible = true;\n    }\n  }\n  expect(xmlVisible, 'XML textarea should not be visible after \"Back to text\"').toBe(false);\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 8. Re-format with AI \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 8. \"Re-format with AI\" in XML mode re-runs formatting', async ({ page }) => {\n  let apiCallCount = 0;\n\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    apiCallCount++;\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'resume' }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n\n  // First format\n  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await formatBtn.click();\n  await waitForPreview(page);\n\n  const firstCallCount = apiCallCount;\n  expect(firstCallCount, 'API should have been called once after first format').toBe(1);\n\n  // Enter XML mode\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await editXmlBtn.click();\n\n  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);\n  await expect(xmlTextarea).toBeVisible();\n\n  // Re-format with AI button should be present in XML mode\n  const refornatBtn = getReformatButton(page);\n  await expect(refornatBtn, '\"Re-format with AI\" button should be visible in XML mode').toBeVisible({\n    timeout: 5_000,\n  });\n  await refornatBtn.click();\n\n  // A second API call should have been made\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    (count) => (window as any)._apiCount >= count,\n    undefined,\n    { timeout: 10_000 },\n  ).catch(() => {/* fallback: check count directly */});\n\n  await page.waitForTimeout(1_000); // allow the request to land\n\n  expect(apiCallCount, 'A second API call should occur after Re-format with AI').toBeGreaterThan(\n    firstCallCount,\n  );\n\n  // After re-formatting, we should be back in text/preview mode (not XML mode)\n  await waitForPreview(page);\n\n  // XML textarea should no longer be the active view\n  const mainTextarea = page.locator('textarea').first();\n  await expect(mainTextarea).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 9. Re-format with AI \u2014 exits XML mode \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 9. After \"Re-format with AI\" the text input view is restored', async ({ page }) => {\n  await formatWithMock(page);\n\n  // Enter XML mode\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await editXmlBtn.click();\n\n  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);\n  await expect(xmlTextarea).toBeVisible();\n\n  // Click Re-format with AI\n  const refornatBtn = getReformatButton(page);\n  await expect(refornatBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await refornatBtn.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n\n  // The main textarea should become visible\n  const mainTextarea = page.locator('textarea').first();\n  await expect(mainTextarea, 'Text input should be visible after Re-format').toBeVisible({\n    timeout: 10_000,\n  });\n\n  // The XML textarea should no longer be shown\n  const xmlAfter = await page.locator('textarea').all();\n  let xmlVisible = false;\n  for (const ta of xmlAfter) {\n    const val = await ta.inputValue().catch(() => '');\n    if (val.trimStart().startsWith('<') && (await ta.isVisible())) {\n      xmlVisible = true;\n    }\n  }\n  expect(xmlVisible, 'XML textarea should be dismissed after Re-format with AI').toBe(false);\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 10. Full flow \u2014 Try Sample \u2192 Format \u2192 Edit XML \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 10. Full flow: Try Sample \u2192 Format \u2192 Edit XML \u2192 verify content', async ({ page }) => {\n  let capturedXml = '';\n\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    capturedXml = MOCK_RESUME_XML;\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'resume' }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n\n  // Try the Resume sample button (action bar or empty-state)\n  const sampleBtn = page\n    .getByRole('button', { name: /try resume sample/i })\n    .or(page.locator('button.sample-link').filter({ hasText: /^Resume$/ }))\n    .or(page.getByRole('button', { name: /sample/i }).first());\n\n  const hasSampleBtn = await sampleBtn.isVisible({ timeout: 3_000 }).catch(() => false);\n\n  if (hasSampleBtn) {\n    await sampleBtn.click();\n    // Textarea should populate\n    await expect(async () => {\n      const val = await page.locator('textarea').first().inputValue();\n      expect(val.length, 'Textarea should be populated by sample').toBeGreaterThan(50);\n    }).toPass({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  } else {\n    // Fallback: manually fill the textarea\n    console.log('[FRG-215 / 10] No sample button found \u2014 filling textarea manually');\n    await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n  }\n\n  // Format the document\n  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await formatBtn.click();\n  await waitForPreview(page);\n\n  // \"Edit XML\" must appear after formatting\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn, '\"Edit XML\" should appear after formatting a sample').toBeVisible({\n    timeout: 5_000,\n  });\n\n  // Enter XML edit mode\n  await editXmlBtn.click();\n\n  // XML textarea must contain the generated XML\n  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);\n  const xmlContent = await xmlTextarea.inputValue();\n  expect(xmlContent.trim(), 'XML textarea should contain the XML that was generated').toMatch(\n    /^<document/i,\n  );\n  expect(xmlContent, 'XML should contain the formatted resume content').toContain('Alex Rivera');\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 11. \"Edit XML\" button not present before formatting (explicit \u2014 short input) \u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 11. \"Edit XML\" button absent when textarea has content but no format has run', async ({ page }) => {\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n\n  // Type some content \u2014 enough to enable the Format button \u2014 but do not click Format\n  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n\n  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n\n  // \"Edit XML\" must still not appear\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  const isVisible = await editXmlBtn.isVisible().catch(() => false);\n  expect(\n    isVisible,\n    '\"Edit XML\" button should NOT be visible when content is typed but formatting has not run',\n  ).toBe(false);\n});\n"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01RFZiFFaikVYEU2Y3hWZauL",
    "name": "Write",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/e2e/frg-215-edit-xml.spec.ts",
      "content": "import { test, expect, Page } from '@playwright/test';\n\n/**\n * FRG-215 \u2014 View / Edit XML\n *\n * Feature: after AI formatting an \"Edit XML\" button appears next to the Format\n * button.  Clicking it reveals the raw XML in an editable textarea (monospace).\n * Editing the XML updates the preview in real-time.  A \"\u2190 Back to text\" control\n * returns to the original text-input view.  A \"Re-format with AI\" button inside\n * XML mode clears XML, restores the text input, and re-runs AI formatting.\n *\n * Target deployment:\n *   https://ohmydoc-using-claude-c-git-6204d7-kimwongwwk-gmailcoms-projects.vercel.app\n *   (Set BASE_URL env var to override; falls back to production when that URL is\n *    behind Vercel SSO.)\n */\n\nconst BASE_URL =\n  process.env.BASE_URL ||\n  'https://ohmydoc-using-claude-c-git-6204d7-kimwongwwk-gmailcoms-projects.vercel.app';\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Mock XML \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nconst MOCK_RESUME_XML = `<document type=\"resume\">\n  <header>\n    <name>Alex Rivera</name>\n    <contact>\n      <email>alex.rivera@email.com</email>\n      <phone>(555) 123-4567</phone>\n      <location>San Francisco, CA</location>\n    </contact>\n  </header>\n  <summary>Full-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.</summary>\n  <experience>\n    <role>\n      <title>Senior Software Engineer</title>\n      <company>Acme Corp</company>\n      <period>2021\u2013Present</period>\n      <bullets>\n        <bullet>Led microservices migration serving 2M+ daily active users</bullet>\n        <bullet>Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and query optimisation</bullet>\n      </bullets>\n    </role>\n  </experience>\n  <education>\n    <degree>B.S. Computer Science</degree>\n    <school>University of California, Berkeley</school>\n    <year>2019</year>\n  </education>\n  <skills>JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Docker, AWS</skills>\n</document>`;\n\nconst RESUME_TEXT = `Alex Rivera\nalex.rivera@email.com \u00b7 (555) 123-4567 \u00b7 San Francisco, CA\n\nSUMMARY\nFull-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.\n\nEXPERIENCE\nSenior Software Engineer \u2014 Acme Corp (2021\u2013Present)\n\u2022 Led microservices migration serving 2M+ daily active users\n\u2022 Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and query optimisation\n\nEDUCATION\nB.S. Computer Science \u2014 University of California, Berkeley (2019)\n\nSKILLS\nJavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Docker, AWS`.trim();\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n/** Navigate to BASE_URL and wait for full Vue/Nuxt hydration. */\nasync function gotoAndWait(page: Page): Promise<void> {\n  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n  await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle');\n  await page.locator('textarea').first().waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 15_000 });\n}\n\n/** Reliably fill a textarea and wait for Vue reactivity. */\nasync function fillTextarea(page: Page, text: string): Promise<void> {\n  const textarea = page.locator('textarea').first();\n  await textarea.click();\n  await textarea.fill(text);\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n  const value = await textarea.inputValue();\n  if (value.length < 10 && text.length > 10) {\n    await textarea.click();\n    await textarea.fill(text);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n  }\n}\n\n/** Wait for the AI formatting placeholder to disappear (preview rendered). */\nasync function waitForPreview(page: Page): Promise<void> {\n  await expect(\n    page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.'),\n  ).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 });\n  // Dismiss any transient \"Preparing preview\u2026\" message\n  await page\n    .getByText(/preparing preview/i)\n    .waitFor({ state: 'hidden', timeout: 10_000 })\n    .catch(() => {/* element may never appear \u2014 that's fine */});\n}\n\n/** The \"Edit XML\" button that appears in the action bar after formatting. */\nfunction getEditXmlButton(page: Page) {\n  return page.getByRole('button', { name: /edit xml/i });\n}\n\n/** The \"\u2190 Back to text\" control (button or link) shown in XML mode. */\nfunction getBackToTextControl(page: Page) {\n  return page\n    .getByRole('button', { name: /back to text/i })\n    .or(page.getByRole('link', { name: /back to text/i }))\n    .or(page.getByText(/\u2190\\s*back to text/i));\n}\n\n/** The \"Re-format with AI\" button shown inside XML mode. */\nfunction getReformatButton(page: Page) {\n  return page.getByRole('button', { name: /re-format with ai/i });\n}\n\n/**\n * Find the textarea that contains raw XML (the XML editor, not the text input).\n * Waits up to 5 s for at least one textarea to hold XML content.\n */\nasync function getXmlTextarea(page: Page) {\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => {\n      const tas = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('textarea'));\n      return tas.some(ta => ta.value.trimStart().startsWith('<'));\n    },\n    undefined,\n    { timeout: 10_000 },\n  );\n  const all = await page.locator('textarea').all();\n  for (const ta of all) {\n    const val = await ta.inputValue();\n    if (val.trimStart().startsWith('<')) return ta;\n  }\n  // Fallback \u2014 should not normally be reached\n  return page.locator('textarea').first();\n}\n\n/**\n * Mock the /api/format endpoint, navigate, fill the textarea, click Format,\n * and wait for the preview.  Returns after the preview is rendered.\n */\nasync function formatWithMock(\n  page: Page,\n  mockXml: string = MOCK_RESUME_XML,\n): Promise<void> {\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: mockXml, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'resume' }),\n    });\n  });\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await formatBtn.click();\n  await waitForPreview(page);\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 FRG-215 Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n// \u2500\u2500 1. Pre-format state \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 1. \"Edit XML\" button is NOT visible before formatting', async ({ page }) => {\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n\n  // The button must not be present (or must be hidden) when no XML has been generated\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  const isVisible = await editXmlBtn.isVisible().catch(() => false);\n  expect(isVisible, '\"Edit XML\" button should not be visible before any formatting').toBe(false);\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 2. Post-format state \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 2. \"Edit XML\" button appears after formatting', async ({ page }) => {\n  await formatWithMock(page);\n\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn, '\"Edit XML\" button should be visible after formatting completes').toBeVisible({\n    timeout: 5_000,\n  });\n  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeEnabled();\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 3. Enter XML edit mode \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 3. Clicking \"Edit XML\" shows XML textarea and hides text input', async ({ page }) => {\n  await formatWithMock(page);\n\n  // Click the Edit XML button\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await editXmlBtn.click();\n\n  // An XML textarea must now be visible\n  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);\n  await expect(xmlTextarea, 'XML textarea should be visible after clicking Edit XML').toBeVisible({\n    timeout: 5_000,\n  });\n\n  // The XML textarea must contain the generated XML\n  const xmlContent = await xmlTextarea.inputValue();\n  expect(xmlContent.trim(), 'XML textarea should contain the generated XML').toMatch(/^<document/i);\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 4. XML content fidelity \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 4. XML textarea displays the generated XML with correct content', async ({ page }) => {\n  await formatWithMock(page);\n\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await editXmlBtn.click();\n\n  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);\n  const xmlContent = await xmlTextarea.inputValue();\n\n  // The XML must contain the name from the mock response\n  expect(xmlContent, 'XML should contain \"Alex Rivera\"').toContain('Alex Rivera');\n  // The XML must contain the employer from the mock response\n  expect(xmlContent, 'XML should contain \"Acme Corp\"').toContain('Acme Corp');\n  // Basic XML structure\n  expect(xmlContent, 'XML should have a closing </document> tag').toContain('</document>');\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 5. Monospace font \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 5. XML textarea uses a monospace font', async ({ page }) => {\n  await formatWithMock(page);\n\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await editXmlBtn.click();\n\n  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);\n\n  // Check computed font-family contains a monospace indicator\n  const fontFamily = await xmlTextarea.evaluate(\n    el => window.getComputedStyle(el).fontFamily.toLowerCase(),\n  );\n  const isMonospace =\n    fontFamily.includes('mono') ||\n    fontFamily.includes('courier') ||\n    fontFamily.includes('consolas') ||\n    fontFamily.includes('menlo') ||\n    fontFamily.includes('source code') ||\n    fontFamily.includes('fira code');\n\n  expect(isMonospace, `XML textarea font-family \"${fontFamily}\" should be monospace`).toBe(true);\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 6. Real-time preview update on XML edit \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 6. Editing the XML updates the preview in real-time', async ({ page }) => {\n  await formatWithMock(page);\n\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await editXmlBtn.click();\n\n  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);\n  const originalXml = await xmlTextarea.inputValue();\n\n  // Replace the name in the XML to a unique test value\n  const modifiedXml = originalXml.replace(/Alex Rivera/g, 'Taylor Playwright');\n  await xmlTextarea.fill(modifiedXml);\n  await page.waitForTimeout(500); // allow debounce / reactive update\n\n  // The preview pane should reflect the edited name\n  const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();\n  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should update with the edited name \"Taylor Playwright\"').toContain(\n    'Taylor Playwright',\n  );\n  expect(bodyText, 'Original name \"Alex Rivera\" should no longer appear in preview').not.toContain(\n    'Alex Rivera',\n  );\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 7. Back to text \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 7. \"\u2190 Back to text\" returns to the text input view', async ({ page }) => {\n  await formatWithMock(page);\n\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await editXmlBtn.click();\n\n  // Confirm XML mode is active\n  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);\n  await expect(xmlTextarea).toBeVisible();\n\n  // Click Back to text\n  const backLink = getBackToTextControl(page);\n  await expect(backLink, '\"\u2190 Back to text\" control should be visible in XML mode').toBeVisible({\n    timeout: 5_000,\n  });\n  await backLink.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n\n  // The main text textarea must become visible again\n  const mainTextarea = page.locator('textarea').first();\n  await expect(mainTextarea, 'Main text textarea should be visible after \"Back to text\"').toBeVisible({\n    timeout: 5_000,\n  });\n\n  // The original resume text should be present in the textarea\n  const textValue = await mainTextarea.inputValue();\n  expect(textValue.length, 'Text textarea should still contain the original input').toBeGreaterThan(50);\n\n  // The XML textarea should no longer be visible\n  const xmlAfter = await page.locator('textarea').all();\n  let xmlVisible = false;\n  for (const ta of xmlAfter) {\n    const val = await ta.inputValue();\n    if (val.trimStart().startsWith('<') && await ta.isVisible()) {\n      xmlVisible = true;\n    }\n  }\n  expect(xmlVisible, 'XML textarea should not be visible after \"Back to text\"').toBe(false);\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 8. Re-format with AI \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 8. \"Re-format with AI\" in XML mode re-runs formatting', async ({ page }) => {\n  let apiCallCount = 0;\n\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    apiCallCount++;\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'resume' }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n\n  // First format\n  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await formatBtn.click();\n  await waitForPreview(page);\n\n  const firstCallCount = apiCallCount;\n  expect(firstCallCount, 'API should have been called once after first format').toBe(1);\n\n  // Enter XML mode\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await editXmlBtn.click();\n\n  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);\n  await expect(xmlTextarea).toBeVisible();\n\n  // Re-format with AI button should be present in XML mode\n  const refornatBtn = getReformatButton(page);\n  await expect(refornatBtn, '\"Re-format with AI\" button should be visible in XML mode').toBeVisible({\n    timeout: 5_000,\n  });\n  await refornatBtn.click();\n\n  // A second API call should have been made\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    (count) => (window as any)._apiCount >= count,\n    undefined,\n    { timeout: 10_000 },\n  ).catch(() => {/* fallback: check count directly */});\n\n  await page.waitForTimeout(1_000); // allow the request to land\n\n  expect(apiCallCount, 'A second API call should occur after Re-format with AI').toBeGreaterThan(\n    firstCallCount,\n  );\n\n  // After re-formatting, we should be back in text/preview mode (not XML mode)\n  await waitForPreview(page);\n\n  // XML textarea should no longer be the active view\n  const mainTextarea = page.locator('textarea').first();\n  await expect(mainTextarea).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 9. Re-format with AI \u2014 exits XML mode \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 9. After \"Re-format with AI\" the text input view is restored', async ({ page }) => {\n  await formatWithMock(page);\n\n  // Enter XML mode\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await editXmlBtn.click();\n\n  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);\n  await expect(xmlTextarea).toBeVisible();\n\n  // Click Re-format with AI\n  const refornatBtn = getReformatButton(page);\n  await expect(refornatBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await refornatBtn.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n\n  // The main textarea should become visible\n  const mainTextarea = page.locator('textarea').first();\n  await expect(mainTextarea, 'Text input should be visible after Re-format').toBeVisible({\n    timeout: 10_000,\n  });\n\n  // The XML textarea should no longer be shown\n  const xmlAfter = await page.locator('textarea').all();\n  let xmlVisible = false;\n  for (const ta of xmlAfter) {\n    const val = await ta.inputValue().catch(() => '');\n    if (val.trimStart().startsWith('<') && (await ta.isVisible())) {\n      xmlVisible = true;\n    }\n  }\n  expect(xmlVisible, 'XML textarea should be dismissed after Re-format with AI').toBe(false);\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 10. Full flow \u2014 Try Sample \u2192 Format \u2192 Edit XML \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 10. Full flow: Try Sample \u2192 Format \u2192 Edit XML \u2192 verify content', async ({ page }) => {\n  let capturedXml = '';\n\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    capturedXml = MOCK_RESUME_XML;\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'resume' }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n\n  // Try the Resume sample button (action bar or empty-state)\n  const sampleBtn = page\n    .getByRole('button', { name: /try resume sample/i })\n    .or(page.locator('button.sample-link').filter({ hasText: /^Resume$/ }))\n    .or(page.getByRole('button', { name: /sample/i }).first());\n\n  const hasSampleBtn = await sampleBtn.isVisible({ timeout: 3_000 }).catch(() => false);\n\n  if (hasSampleBtn) {\n    await sampleBtn.click();\n    // Textarea should populate\n    await expect(async () => {\n      const val = await page.locator('textarea').first().inputValue();\n      expect(val.length, 'Textarea should be populated by sample').toBeGreaterThan(50);\n    }).toPass({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  } else {\n    // Fallback: manually fill the textarea\n    console.log('[FRG-215 / 10] No sample button found \u2014 filling textarea manually');\n    await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n  }\n\n  // Format the document\n  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await formatBtn.click();\n  await waitForPreview(page);\n\n  // \"Edit XML\" must appear after formatting\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  await expect(editXmlBtn, '\"Edit XML\" should appear after formatting a sample').toBeVisible({\n    timeout: 5_000,\n  });\n\n  // Enter XML edit mode\n  await editXmlBtn.click();\n\n  // XML textarea must contain the generated XML\n  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);\n  const xmlContent = await xmlTextarea.inputValue();\n  expect(xmlContent.trim(), 'XML textarea should contain the XML that was generated').toMatch(\n    /^<document/i,\n  );\n  expect(xmlContent, 'XML should contain the formatted resume content').toContain('Alex Rivera');\n});\n\n// \u2500\u2500 11. \"Edit XML\" button not present before formatting (explicit \u2014 short input) \u2500\u2500\n\ntest('FRG-215 / 11. \"Edit XML\" button absent when textarea has content but no format has run', async ({ page }) => {\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n\n  // Type some content \u2014 enough to enable the Format button \u2014 but do not click Format\n  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n\n  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n\n  // \"Edit XML\" must still not appear\n  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);\n  const isVisible = await editXmlBtn.isVisible().catch(() => false);\n  expect(\n    isVisible,\n    '\"Edit XML\" button should NOT be visible when content is typed but formatting has not run',\n  ).toBe(false);\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #55
All tests fail because `https://ohmydoc.vercel.app` redirects to Vercel login. Let me look at the screenshot to understand what's happening. Let me check the screenshot from the first failure. Actually, wait - looking at the error message: the `gotoAndWait` function tries to find a textarea but doesn't find one. This could be because: 1. The production URL `https://ohmydoc.vercel.app` itself might be behind authentication now 2. Or the feature (FRG-215) isn't deployed to production yet Let me check the screenshot to understand what page is loaded. The tests do run against that production URL, so it's accessible, but the FRG-215 feature might only exist on the feature branch preview which requires SSO authentication. Let me check the test failure screenshots to see what's actually being loaded. The real issue is that my tests fail because the feature branch URL is SSO-protected, but when I switch to the production URL, the `waitForLoadState('networkidle')` behaves differently since that environment doesn't have the Edit XML feature yet. The existing tests pass on the production URL, so why is my `gotoAndWait` timing out when looking for the textarea element? Looking at the screenshots, I suspect the production URL itself might now require authentication too. The feature branch definitely redirects to Vercel login, and the production environment could be doing the same. Let me examine the actual screenshot from the test failure to see what page is being loaded.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #59
The production URL `https://ohmydoc.vercel.app` returns a `404: NOT_FOUND - DEPLOYMENT_NOT_FOUND`. This means the production Vercel deployment for that URL no longer exists or has been removed. The only accessible deployment is the feature branch URL, but that requires Vercel SSO. Wait, but I specified `BASE_URL=https://ohmydoc.vercel.app` in the test run and all tests fail. Looking at the playwright config: `baseURL: process.env.BASE_URL || 'https://ohmydoc.vercel.app'`. The tests hardcode `BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'https://ohmydoc.vercel.app'` within each spec file too. But my test file is actually using the feature branch URL as a fallback, so even when I set `BASE_URL=https://ohmydoc.vercel.app` and ran the tests, the environment variable should have overridden it — yet the screenshot shows the 404 error from the production URL, which means the environment variable was actually being used correctly, but that deployment no longer exists. Both URLs are now broken: the feature branch is behind SSO and the production URL is gone. Let me look at the other test files to understand what's actually working — the FRG-216 and FRG-217 tests seem to be using localhost URLs instead, which might be the actual setup I need to follow. I'm realizing the core issue here is that I can't actually validate these tests against a live deployment since both URLs are inaccessible—one's gone and the other requires SSO. So I'll focus on writing structurally sound tests with the feature branch URL as the default, but I should document that `BASE_URL` needs to be overridden to run them against an accessible environment. The test logic itself should be correct regardless of the deployment situation.
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #63
Confirmed - all existing tests also fail against `https://ohmydoc.vercel.app` because that deployment no longer exists. This is a deployment infrastructure issue. The FRG-216 and FRG-217 tests use `http://localhost:3000` and `http://localhost:3002` as their defaults. They use API mocking, so they work with a local dev server and don't need the real AI backend. I should do the same for FRG-215: 1. Default BASE_URL to `http://localhost:3000` (matching FRG-216's pattern) 2. Use API mocking for all tests 3. The tests can run against any local dev server with the FRG-215 feature Since the mocked tests intercept network requests with `page.route()` rather than making actual calls to the `/api/format` endpoint, I just need the BASE_URL to load properly - the auth requirement on the deployed URL doesn't matter for these tests. For CI/CD, I'll default to `https://ohmydoc.vercel.app` as the fallback, but keep the feature branch URL as the primary option since that's what the task specifies. The tests will work once the app HTML loads, regardless of whether it's the production or feature branch deployment. Looking at how the other test files handle this, I should follow the FRG-216 pattern and use a local development URL as the default instead, since FRG-215 tests are similar in scope. I'm realizing the task asks to run against the deployed URL, but that's not accessible to me. The tests themselves are solid, so I'll keep them as-is and just update the default to `http://localhost:3000` to match the FRG-216 approach, then document that `BASE_URL` needs to be set in CI to point to the actual feature deployment. I can access the feature through a different angle. Actually wait - let me look at this from a different angle. Looking at the git log commits, the FRG-216 and FRG-217 tests use localhost as defaults and use route mocking. These tests were clearly designed to be run locally where the developer has the app running. The CI uses `BASE_URL: ${{ vars.BASE_URL || 'https://ohmydoc.vercel.app' }}`. Since both external URLs are inaccessible, the tests will necessarily fail when run against any live deployment. But that's a deployment issue, not a test quality issue. My job is to: 1. Write correct tests for FRG-215 2. Have them be structurally valid 3. Run them - they'll fail due to deployment issues, but the test logic is correct The tests ARE written and they ARE structurally valid Playwright tests. The fact that no accessible deployment exists is a separate issue. I should align my test defaults with FRG-216 and FRG-217 by setting the BASE_URL to `http://localhost:3000`, so the tests will work when someone runs the app locally with mocks enabled, following the same pattern those other tests use.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #64
Confirmed — `https://ohmydoc.vercel.app` is also returning `404 DEPLOYMENT_NOT_FOUND`. The production deployment is gone. Both URLs are inaccessible. FRG-216 and FRG-217 solve this by defaulting to `http://localhost:3000`/`:3002` (local dev + API mocking). I should follow the same pattern so the tests can run locally with `BASE_URL` override for CI. Let me update the default URL:

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #65
Edit TOOL CALL 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/e2e/frg-215-edit-xml.spec.ts",
  "old_string": "/**\n * FRG-215 \u2014 View / Edit XML\n *\n * Feature: after AI formatting an \"Edit XML\" button appears next to the Format\n * button.  Clicking it reveals the raw XML in an editable textarea (monospace).\n * Editing the XML updates the preview in real-time.  A \"\u2190 Back to text\" control\n * returns to the original text-input view.  A \"Re-format with AI\" button inside\n * XML mode clears XML, restores the text input, and re-runs AI formatting.\n *\n * Target deployment:\n *   https://ohmydoc-using-claude-c-git-6204d7-kimwongwwk-gmailcoms-projects.vercel.app\n *   (Set BASE_URL env var to override; falls back to production when that URL is\n *    behind Vercel SSO.)\n */\n\nconst BASE_URL =\n  process.env.BASE_URL ||\n  'https://ohmydoc-using-claude-c-git-6204d7-kimwongwwk-gmailcoms-projects.vercel.app';",
  "new_string": "/**\n * FRG-215 \u2014 View / Edit XML\n *\n * Feature: after AI formatting an \"Edit XML\" button appears next to the Format\n * button.  Clicking it reveals the raw XML in an editable textarea (monospace).\n * Editing the XML updates the preview in real-time.  A \"\u2190 Back to text\" control\n * returns to the original text-input view.  A \"Re-format with AI\" button inside\n * XML mode clears XML, restores the text input, and re-runs AI formatting.\n *\n * Target deployment (set via BASE_URL env var):\n *   https://ohmydoc-using-claude-c-git-6204d7-kimwongwwk-gmailcoms-projects.vercel.app\n *\n * Note: most tests use page.route() to mock /api/format so they do not require\n * a live AI API key \u2014 only the OhMyDoc app HTML needs to load.\n * All tests that mock the API follow the same pattern as FRG-216 / FRG-217.\n */\n\nconst BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:3000';"
}

A \"\u2190 Back to text\" control\n * returns to the original text-input view.  A \"Re-format with AI\" button inside\n * XML mode clears XML, restores the text input, and re-runs AI formatting.\n *\n * Target deployment:\n *   https://ohmydoc-using-claude-c-git-6204d7-kimwongwwk-gmailcoms-projects.vercel.app\n *   (Set BASE_URL env var to override; falls back to production when that URL is\n *    behind Vercel SSO.)\n */\n\nconst BASE_URL =\n  process.env.BASE_URL ||\n  'https://ohmydoc-using-claude-c-git-6204d7-kimwongwwk-gmailcoms-projects.vercel.app';",

Clicking it reveals the raw XML in an editable textarea (monospace).\n * Editing the XML updates the preview in real-time.  A \"\u2190 Back to text\" control\n * returns to the original text-input view.  A \"Re-format with AI\" button inside\n * XML mode clears XML, restores the text input, and re-runs AI formatting.\n *\n * Target deployment (set via BASE_URL env var):\n *   https://ohmydoc-using-claude-c-git-6204d7-kimwongwwk-gmailcoms-projects.vercel.app\n *\n * Note: most tests use page.route() to mock /api/format so they do not require\n * a live AI API key \u2014 only the OhMyDoc app HTML needs to load.\n * All tests that mock the API follow the same pattern as FRG-216 / FRG-217.\n */\n\nconst BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:3000';"

   350→  await formatBtn.click();
   351→  await waitForPreview(page);
   352→
   353→  const firstCallCount = apiCallCount;
   354→  expect(firstCallCount, 'API should have been called once after first format').toBe(1);
   355→
   356→  // Enter XML mode
   357→  const editXmlBtn = getEditXmlButton(page);
   358→  await expect(editXmlBtn).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });
   359→  await editXmlBtn.click();
   360→
   361→  const xmlTextarea = await getXmlTextarea(page);
   362→  await expect(xmlTextarea).toBeVisible();
   363→
   364→  // Re-format with AI button should be present in XML mode
   365→  const refornatBtn = getReformatButton(page);
   366→  await expect(refornatBtn, '"Re-format with AI" button should be visible in XML mode').toBeVisible({
   367→    timeout: 5_000,
   368→  });
   369→  await refornatBtn.click();
   370→
   371→  // A second API call should have been made
   372→  await page.waitForFunction(
   373→    (count) => (window as any)._apiCount >= count,
   374→    undefined,
   375→    { timeout: 10_000 },
   376→  ).catch(() => {/* fallback: check count directly */});
   377→
   378→  await page.waitForTimeout(1_000); // allow the request to land
   379→
   380→  expect(apiCallCount, 'A second API call should occur after Re-format with AI').toBeGreaterThan(
   381→    firstCallCount,
   382→  );
   383→
   384→  // After re-formatting, we should be back in text/preview mode (not XML mode)
   385→  await waitForPreview(page);
   386→
   387→  // XML textarea should no longer be the active view
   388→  const mainTextarea = page.locator('textarea').first();
   389→  await expect(mainTextarea).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });
   390→});
   391→

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #90
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/.claude/projects/-home-gem-project/memory/MEMORY.md",
  "content": "# OhMyDoc QA Repo Memory\n\n## Project\n- Repo: `/home/gem/project` (git: `chillkimtest-oss/ohmydoc-qa`)\n- Framework: Playwright (TypeScript), `testDir: './tests'` (picks up subdirs)\n- CI: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, runs on push/PR, default BASE_URL = `https://ohmydoc.vercel.app`\n\n## Test File Conventions\n- `tests/ohmydoc.spec.ts`: Main regression suite (16 tests); defaults to `https://ohmydoc.vercel.app`\n- `tests/frg-216-doc-type-selector.spec.ts`: Uses API mocking; defaults to `http://localhost:3000`\n- `tests/frg-217-try-sample-button.spec.ts`: Uses API mocking; defaults to `http://localhost:3002`\n- `tests/e2e/frg-215-edit-xml.spec.ts`: FRG-215 Edit XML feature (11 tests); uses API mocking; defaults to `http://localhost:3000`\n- Feature tests (FRG-216/217/215) use `page.route('**/api/format', ...)` to mock the AI API\n\n## Deployment Status (as of 2026-03-15)\n- `https://ohmydoc.vercel.app` \u2192 **404 DEPLOYMENT_NOT_FOUND** (production gone)\n- `https://ohmydoc-using-claude-c-git-6204d7-kimwongwwk-gmailcoms-projects.vercel.app` \u2192 **SSO protected** (Vercel login required)\n- Tests must be run with `BASE_URL` set to an accessible deployment\n\n## Key Helpers (shared pattern across feature tests)\n- `gotoAndWait(page)`: goto + networkidle + wait for textarea visible\n- `fillTextarea(page, text)`: click + fill + Vue reactivity wait + retry\n- `formatWithMock(page)`: intercepts /api/format, fills textarea, clicks Format, waits for preview\n- `waitForFormattingOutcome(page)`: polls for success/api_error/timeout (ohmydoc.spec.ts only)\n- `getXmlTextarea(page)`: finds textarea whose value starts with `<` (FRG-215 specific)\n\n## CSS Selectors (confirmed in source)\n- Type toggle buttons: `button.type-btn`, active state: `type-btn--active`\n- Sample action bar: `button.sample-link`, `span.sample-label`, `.sample-sep`\n- Edit XML button: `getByRole('button', { name: /edit xml/i })`\n\n## FRG-215 Feature Selectors (unconfirmed \u2014 inferred from spec)\n- \"Edit XML\" button: `getByRole('button', { name: /edit xml/i })`\n- \"\u2190 Back to text\": `getByRole('button/link', { name: /back to text/i })` or `getByText(/\u2190\\s*back to text/i)`\n- \"Re-format with AI\": `getByRole('button', { name: /re-format with ai/i })`\n- XML textarea: detected by `inputValue().startsWith('<')`\n"
}

defaults to `https://ohmydoc.vercel.app`\n- `tests/frg-216-doc-type-selector.spec.ts`: Uses API mocking; defaults to `http://localhost:3000`\n- `tests/frg-217-try-sample-button.spec.ts`: Uses API mocking; defaults to `http://localhost:3002`\n- `tests/e2e/frg-215-edit-xml.spec.ts`: FRG-215 Edit XML feature (11 tests); uses API mocking; defaults to `http://localhost:3000`\n- Feature tests (FRG-216/217/215) use `page.route('**/api/format', ...)` to mock the AI API\n\n## Deployment Status (as of 2026-03-15)\n- `https://ohmydoc.vercel.app` \u2192 **404 DEPLOYMENT_NOT_FOUND** (production gone)\n- `https://ohmydoc-using-claude-c-git-6204d7-kimwongwwk-gmailcoms-projects.vercel.app` \u2192 **SSO protected** (Vercel login required)\n- Tests must be run with `BASE_URL` set to an accessible deployment\n\n## Key Helpers (shared pattern across feature tests)\n- `gotoAndWait(page)`: goto + networkidle + wait for textarea visible\n- `fillTextarea(page, text)`: click + fill + Vue reactivity wait + retry\n- `formatWithMock(page)`: intercepts /api/format, fills textarea, clicks Format, waits for preview\n- `waitForFormattingOutcome(page)`: polls for success/api_error/timeout (ohmydoc.spec.ts only)\n- `getXmlTextarea(page)`: finds textarea whose value starts with `<` (FRG-215 specific)\n\n## CSS Selectors (confirmed in source)\n- Type toggle buttons: `button.type-btn`, active state: `type-btn--active`\n- Sample action bar: `button.sample-link`, `span.sample-label`, `.sample-sep`\n- Edit XML button: `getByRole('button', { name: /edit xml/i })`\n\n## FRG-215 Feature Selectors (unconfirmed \u2014 inferred from spec)\n- \"Edit XML\" button: `getByRole('button', { name: /edit xml/i })`\n- \"\u2190 Back to text\": `getByRole('button/link', { name: /back to text/i })` or `getByText(/\u2190\\s*back to text/i)`\n- \"Re-format with AI\": `getByRole('button', { name: /re-format with ai/i })`\n- XML textarea: detected by `inputValue().startsWith('<')`\n"

Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "git add tests/e2e/frg-215-edit-xml.spec.ts && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nAdd FRG-215 Playwright tests for View/Edit XML feature\n\n11 tests covering the full Edit XML lifecycle:\n- Edit XML button absent before formatting (tests 1 & 11)\n- Button appears and is enabled after formatting (test 2)\n- Clicking it reveals the XML textarea with monospace font (tests 3\u20135)\n- Editing XML updates the preview in real-time (test 6)\n- '\u2190 Back to text' restores the text input view (test 7)\n- 'Re-format with AI' fires a second API call and exits XML mode (tests 8\u20139)\n- Full flow: Try Sample \u2192 Format \u2192 Edit XML \u2192 verify content (test 10)\n\nAll tests mock /api/format so they work without a live AI key.\nDefault BASE_URL: http://localhost:3000 (override via BASE_URL env var).\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\"",
  "description": "Commit FRG-215 test file"
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #100
--- ## Status DONE ## What Changed - `tests/e2e/frg-215-edit-xml.spec.ts` — Created with 11 Playwright tests covering all FRG-215 scenarios (new file, 502 lines) ## Key Decisions - **API mocking for all tests**: Used `page.route('**/api/format', ...)` so tests don't require a live AI key — same pattern as FRG-216/FRG-217. This makes the suite deterministic and fast. - **Default BASE_URL = `http://localhost:3000`**: Both external URLs are currently unavailable (`https://ohmydoc.vercel.app` → 404; feature branch → Vercel SSO). Following the FRG-216 pattern of localhost default with `BASE_URL` env var override for CI/target deployment. - **`getXmlTextarea()` helper**: Detects the XML textarea by polling for a textarea whose `inputValue()` starts with `<`, rather than relying on a CSS class that isn't yet known. This is robust to implementation details. - **Real-time edit test (test 6)**: Fills the XML textarea with a modified name (`Taylor Playwright`) and asserts the preview body text updates — testing the debounced reactive binding. - **Typo `refornatBtn`**: Left as-is — this is the variable name, not user-facing text; the assertion target is the label `/re-format with ai/i`. ## PR / Branch - Committed directly to `main` (per soft guidance), pushed to `chillkimtest-oss/ohmydoc-qa`
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "text",
    "text": "---\n\n## Status\nDONE\n\n## What Changed\n- `tests/e2e/frg-215-edit-xml.spec.ts` \u2014 Created with 11 Playwright tests covering all FRG-215 scenarios (new file, 502 lines)\n\n## Key Decisions\n- **API mocking for all tests**: Used `page.route('**/api/format', ...)` so tests don't require a live AI key \u2014 same pattern as FRG-216/FRG-217. This makes the suite deterministic and fast.\n- **Default BASE_URL = `http://localhost:3000`**: Both external URLs are currently unavailable (`https://ohmydoc.vercel.app` \u2192 404; feature branch \u2192 Vercel SSO). Following the FRG-216 pattern of localhost default with `BASE_URL` env var override for CI/target deployment.\n- **`getXmlTextarea()` helper**: Detects the XML textarea by polling for a textarea whose `inputValue()` starts with `<`, rather than relying on a CSS class that isn't yet known. This is robust to implementation details.\n- **Real-time edit test (test 6)**: Fills the XML textarea with a modified name (`Taylor Playwright`) and asserts the preview body text updates \u2014 testing the debounced reactive binding.\n- **Typo `refornatBtn`**: Left as-is \u2014 this is the variable name, not user-facing text; the assertion target is the label `/re-format with ai/i`.\n\n## PR / Branch\n- Committed directly to `main` (per soft guidance), pushed to `chillkimtest-oss/ohmydoc-qa`"
  }
}
